quarkusio/quarkus#50126.body:
### Description

I was playing with the HQL console in dev-mode. It's super useful, but I'm hitting two nag points:

## Need to set dev.quarkus.hibernate-orm.dev-ui.allow-hql=true before boot
I didn't know about the requirement of setting this property; the UI is very helpfully suggesting "Shall I add this for you", but then when live-reload kicks in, it seems to forget that this property was set already.

It's literally still present in the `application.properties` (at this point), but I'm not allowed to progress unless I allow it to add it again.

If I quit dev-mode and start over with the property set in the application.properties, then it's fine.

It's a very minor thing, but disqualified the UI from being shown in demos, which is a shame :/

## Unable to use the Enter key
After having typed the query in the appropriate line, hitting the "Enter" key seems natural but it's not bound. I have to go find the mouse and click on the button, this really detracts from the user experience.

I was using our standard quickstart quarkus-quickstarts/hibernate-orm-quickstart .

### Implementation ideas

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50126.title: HQL Console improvements

quarkusio/quarkus#50138.body:
Hibernate Search processor always adds the named qualifier:

https://github.com/yrodiere/quarkus/blob/888266f11c87a961fc9ae28c725f71b94fc2b475/extensions/hibernate-search-orm-elasticsearch/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/search/orm/elasticsearch/deployment/HibernateSearchElasticsearchCdiProcessor.java#L68-L69

while Hibernate ORM:

https://github.com/yrodiere/quarkus/blob/888266f11c87a961fc9ae28c725f71b94fc2b475/extensions/hibernate-orm/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/orm/deployment/HibernateOrmCdiProcessor.java#L350-L353

adds them conditionally. Let's be consistent and always add them.

- See also https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/48783#discussion_r2339993189
quarkusio/quarkus#50138.title: Be more consistent about the CDI qualifiers we apply to Hibernate Search/ORM beans

quarkusio/quarkus#50140.body:
### Describe the bug

Hi, 

i'm using PgPool.pool(...) at the moment to create new pools from code (dynamic tenants). Since PgPool was deprecated i updated my code to use io.vertx.mutiny.sqlclient.Pool this works fine in the Java version. 
`Pool.pool(vertx, PgConnectOptions.fromUri(reactiveBaseUrl + finalDataBase).setUser(username)
					.setPassword(password).setCachePreparedStatements(true), options);`

However in the native compilation i get an error "No implementations of interface io.vertx.sqlclient.spi.Driver found that accept connection options io.vertx.pgclient.PgConnectOptions@7a4d8706".

I also tried it with SqlConnectOptions but had the same result.

### Expected behavior

If quarkus-reactive-pg-client is present in the build the driver should be made discoverable if a pool is constructed via Pool.pool(...)

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create quarkus app with quarkus-reactive-pg-client
2. Call Pool.pool to create a new pool from code


### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Windows 11/Ubuntu server

### Output of `java -version`

Temurin JDK 21 latest

### Mandrel or GraalVM version (if different from Java)

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.23.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

 What's a bit weird to me is that i also have an injected Pool (for my standard connection) using postgres via application.propperties config and that works. So the driver is present in general. I also tried using PgBuilder directly but that gives me the same error.
quarkusio/quarkus#50140.title: io.vertx.mutiny.sqlclient.Pool.pool(...) doesn't find the (postgres) db driver in native compilation being called from code

quarkusio/quarkus#50141.body:
### Describe the bug

With PR https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/48130 the usage of the class `CommunityDatabase` got introduce in the [HibernateProcessorUtil#L241](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/extensions/hibernate-orm/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/orm/deployment/util/HibernateProcessorUtil.java#L241)

When building a project with no default datasource (multi tenancy, in which we provide the tenant datasources at runtime), this causes
```
	[error]: Build step io.quarkus.hibernate.orm.deployment.HibernateOrmProcessor#configurationDescriptorBuilding threw an exception: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: org/hibernate/community/dialect/CommunityDatabase
        	at io.quarkus.hibernate.orm.deployment.util.HibernateProcessorUtil.lambda$supportedDatabaseKindFromProductName$2(HibernateProcessorUtil.java:241)
        	at java.base/java.util.Optional.flatMap(Optional.java:289)
        	at io.quarkus.hibernate.orm.deployment.util.HibernateProcessorUtil.supportedDatabaseKindFromProductName(HibernateProcessorUtil.java:236)
        	at io.quarkus.hibernate.orm.deployment.util.HibernateProcessorUtil.lambda$determineDatabaseKind$0(HibernateProcessorUtil.java:228)
```

The reason for this seems that no `JdbcDataSourceBuildItem` with `default = true` exists, of which the db kind can be read from.
This causes the `dbKindOptional` [HibernateProcessorUtil.java#L226](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/extensions/hibernate-orm/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/orm/deployment/util/HibernateProcessorUtil.java#L226) to be empty, hence calling the method [`HibernateProcessorUtil.supportedDatabaseKindFromProductName`](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/extensions/hibernate-orm/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/orm/deployment/util/HibernateProcessorUtil.java#L231). This again will cause the `NoClassDefFoundError` as, without the `hibernate-orm-derby` extension, the class `CommunityDatabase` is not provided (current providing of the `hibernate-community-dialects` might be probably an issue itself)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.26.4

### Expected behaviour
Without a default datasource, the db kind still should be read from the configuration property
quarkusio/quarkus#50141.title: NoClassDefFoundError: org/hibernate/community/dialect/CommunityDatabase when no default datasource

quarkusio/quarkus#50206.body: ### Description We initialize an implicit converter for Boolean in InitRuntimeConfig: ``` Converter BOOLEAN_CONVERTER = Converters.getImplicitConverter(Boolean.class); ``` which is kinda slow. From what I can see, maybe we can entirely drop it as the TCK challenge got resolved so maybe we can rely on the built in converters directly now? /cc @radcortez ### Implementation ideas _No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer:

```
git clone git@github.com:michalvavrik/quarkus-test-suite.git -b reproducer/dev-mode-hibernate-offline-startup
cd quarkus-test-suite/hibernate/hibernate-offline-startup
mvn clean verify -Dit.test=DevModeHibernateOfflineStartupIT
```

For standalone Quarkus app without PostgreSQL, use:

```
git clone git@github.com:michalvavrik/hibernate-offline-startup-dev-mode-reproducer.git
cd hibernate-offline-startup-dev-mode-reproducer/
quarkus dev
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Fedora 41

### Output of `java -version`

Temurin 21

### Quarkus version or git rev

999-SNAPSHOT

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Maven 3.9.9

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#50225.body:
### Describe the bug

The build fails if there's a method that accepts a parameter with primitive type and the parameter is annotated with a type annotation and this method is called via `jakarta.enterprise.invoke.Invoker` API.

```java
import jakarta.validation.constraints.Min;

void ping(@Min(5) int price) { // -> build fails if it's invoked via the Invoker API
}
```

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

A NPE is thrown from the `InvokerGenerator`:
```
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "java.util.Set.iterator()" because the return value of "java.util.Map.get(Object)" is null
    at io.quarkus.arc.processor.InvokerGenerator.assignWithUnboxingAndWideningConversion(InvokerGenerator.java:407)
    at io.quarkus.arc.processor.InvokerGenerator.createInvokerClass(InvokerGenerator.java:298)
    at io.quarkus.arc.processor.InvokerGenerator.generate(InvokerGenerator.java:93)
    at io.quarkus.arc.processor.BeanProcessor$6.call(BeanProcessor.java:411)
    at io.quarkus.arc.processor.BeanProcessor$6.call(BeanProcessor.java:408)
    at java.base/java.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:317)
    at org.jboss.threads.ContextHandler$1.runWith(ContextHandler.java:18)
    at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2675)
    at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2654)
    at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1591)
```

quarkusio/quarkus#50226.body:
### Describe the bug

The Micrometer HTTP server requests metric is supposed to capture all 404s under a generic `NOT_FOUND` URI tag to avoid cardinality explosion. However, if the not found URL ends with a trailing slash, the URL is separately tagged (with the slash removed).

This also similarly impacts the suppression of other 4xxs. If the URL responding with a 4xx ends with a trailing slash the request is not suppressed in the metrics.

### Expected behavior

All 404s are captured with the generic `NOT_FOUND` URI. Other 4xxs are always suppressed (if so configured).

### Actual behavior

Some URLs sneak through.

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer: [quarkus-reproducer.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22487497/quarkus-reproducer.zip)

**Steps to reproduce**

1. Run the application.
2. Navigate to http://localhost:8080/foo/.
3. Navigate to http://localhost:8080/q/metrics.

**Expected result**

The request is included in the count of `NOT_FOUND` tagged URIs.

**Actual result**

A separate count is given for `uri="/foo"`:

```
http_server_requests_seconds_count{method="GET",outcome="CLIENT_ERROR",status="404",uri="/foo",} 1.0
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Darwin Rods-MacBook-Pro.local 24.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 24.6.0: Mon Aug 11 21:11:04 PDT 2025; root:xnu-11417.140.69.701.11~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6031 arm64

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "21.0.7" 2025-04-15 OpenJDK Runtime Environment Homebrew (build 21.0.7) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Homebrew (build 21.0.7, mixed mode, sharing)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.25.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Gradle 8.14.1

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50226.title: Micrometer HTTP server requests metrics tags multiple URLs for 404s

### Expected behavior

No failure

### Actual behavior

Build complains about missing datasource definition which shouldn't be necessary at all.

### How to Reproduce?

quarkusio/quarkus#50265.body:
### Describe the bug

For Quarkus 3.28.1, CI reports `j,u.z.ZipException: duplicate entry...` errors for every `quarkusBuild` Gradle task execution. 3.27.0 looks fine.

[Renovate Quarkus 3.28.1 bump PR](https://github.com/apache/polaris/pull/2648). Can be reproduced locally as well.

The issue is _not_ particularly in the `quarkusBuild` task implementation, but raised in a [custom Gradle code](https://github.com/apache/polaris/blob/091fe6f6a269528d95e4d43f7aa2ef90f90cd093/build-logic/src/main/kotlin/Utilities.kt#L76-L106) to make the `quarkus-run.jar`, `generated-bytecode.jar` and `transformed-bytecode.jar` files a (bit more) reproducible by re-recreating those with a fixed file modification timestamp for all zip entries.

However, the exception is thrown because there is a duplicate entry in at least one of the jars created by Quarkus.

In the particular case it is `duplicate entry: io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$$CMImpl.class`.

Reproducer: checkout the renovate-PR branch, run `./gradlew :polaris-server:quarkusBuild --stacktrace`.

The above works fine up to Quarkus 3.27.0.

Noting: the `ZipException` is _not_ raised from Quarkus code, but our custom code, but the duplicate ZIP entry in the Quarkus generated jar is real.

From `generated-bytecode.jar`:
```
     6018  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$$CMImpl.class
     6018  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$$CMImpl.class
     2775  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$KeyCert$$CMImpl.class
     2775  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$KeyCert$$CMImpl.class
     3579  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$ProxyConfig$$CMImpl.class
     3579  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$ProxyConfig$$CMImpl.class
     2565  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$TrustCert$$CMImpl.class
     2565  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterConfig$TrustCert$$CMImpl.class
     6026  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterLogsConfig$$CMImpl.class
     6556  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterMetricsConfig$$CMImpl.class
    10065  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterRuntimeConfig$$CMImpl.class
     6030  1970-01-02 01:00   io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/config/runtime/exporter/OtlpExporterTracesConfig$$CMImpl.class
```

(Noticed the hard-coded modification timestamp, which is nice to see :) )

quarkusio/quarkus#50293.body:
### Describe the bug

If you set quarkus.http.proxy.enable-forwarded-prefix=true and specify an X-Forwarded-Prefix header, that will not be reflected in getBaseUri - but will be present in the UriInfo.getRequestUri and UriInfo.getAbsolutePath.

### Expected behavior

getBaseUri should have the prefix pre-pended.

### Actual behavior

The prefix is omitted.

### How to Reproduce?

With:

quarkus.http.proxy.proxy-address-forwarding=true
quarkus.http.proxy.allow-x-forwarded=true
quarkus.http.proxy.enable-forwarded-host=true
quarkus.http.proxy.enable-forwarded-prefix=true

Submit a request with the header "X-Forwarded-Prefix: prefix" and observe the base uri for the request does not reflect the prefix.


### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.27.0

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

This is a follow-up to #46313

> Because the agent generates a reachability-metadata.json and the agent integration expects to find the older set of json files.
>
> I would be nice it it could read the reachability-metadata.json and still generate the old style files since the newer graalvm version can still read the old style files.

### Expected behavior

Tracing agent integration working with newer GraalVM / Mandrel versions

### Actual behavior

Tracing agent integration is broken

Additionally, starting with GraalVM / Mandrel 25.0 we will also need to use the same JDK version when using the agent (e.g. we can't run the agent with JDK 21 and build the project with Mandrel 25.0) see https://github.com/graalvm/mandrel/issues/893

quarkusio/quarkus#50315.body:
### Describe the bug

# Native Image Issues with OIDC Redis Token State Manager

## Summary

The `quarkus-oidc-redis-token-state-manager` seems to be having some issues with Native Builds (but works fine with normal JVM). 

[Create a repo with some example code that is breaking](https://github.com/Jaland/quarkus-oidc-redis-cache-example)

Initially, the native image failed to start. After applying changes from [this commit](https://github.com/Jaland/quarkus-oidc-redis-cache-example/commit/f04997506aeba368576e7005998ed1cc4b631022), the application now starts but gets stuck in an infinite redirect loop. When the OAuth server redirects back to the application, the application immediately redirects back to the OAuth server.

## Environment

- **Quarkus Version**: 3.26.3  
- **Java Version**: 17
- **Build Tool**: Maven 3.8+
- **OS**: Linux (tested on Fedora 42)
- **OAuth Provider**: Google OAuth 2.0
- **Token Storage**: Redis 7

The OIDC Redis token state manager should work in native images without requiring:
- Manual reflection registration for internal classes
- Jackson workaround flags  
- Additional build arguments

These should be automatically handled by the extension when building native images.

## Additional Context

This issue makes the `quarkus-oidc-redis-token-state-manager` extension unusable in production native deployments. The authentication loop suggests that token serialization/deserialization in Redis may not be working correctly in native images, even with the attempted reflection configuration.

The fact that the same configuration works perfectly in JVM mode indicates this is specifically a native image compatibility issue within the OIDC Redis integration.


### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#50317.body:
### Describe the bug

hello,

in quarkus 3.15.6.1, I got the following controller:

```java    
    @Blocking
    @GET
    @Path("download")
    @Produces(MediaType.APPLICATION_OCTET_STREAM)
    public Response download(@QueryParam("directory") String directory, @QueryParam("file") String filePath) throws IOException {
        java.nio.file.Path root = getDirectory(directory).normalize().toRealPath();
        java.nio.file.Path finalPath = root.resolve(filePath).normalize().toRealPath();

        // security check: avoid attackers to access arbitrary files in the filesystem
        if (!finalPath.startsWith(root)) {
            return Response.status(Response.Status.NOT_FOUND).build();
        }

        String fileName = String.valueOf(finalPath.getFileName());
    
        StreamingOutput responseBody = outputStream -> streamFile(finalPath, outputStream);
        return Response.ok(responseBody)
                .header("Content-Disposition", "attachment; filename=\"" + fileName + "\"")
                .build();
    }

    private static void streamFile(java.nio.file.Path file, OutputStream outputStream) throws IOException {
        try (InputStream inputStream = new BufferedInputStream(Files.newInputStream(file))) {
            inputStream.transferTo(outputStream);
        }
    }
```

It works great for small files but it fails when I'm trying to download a 6GB file:


IIRC, in previous version of quarkus, this method was able to download big files but I cannot remember exactly which version.  This happens on Linux... now I'm going to test on Windows 11.

I switched to using Quarkus for my applications (from Spring Boot), but I'm having trouble because I can't figure out why everything I had configured regarding the appearance of the logs isn't working. Let me try to explain better. In my Spring projects, I had configured Logback + Spring Cloud Logging Lib + Spring Cloud Trace Lib to allow me to configure, based on the active Spring profile, how to append logs once deployed on Google Cloud Run. I have seen that Quarkus has attempted to standardise the use of logs using Jboss Log Manager, and that would be fine with me, but I would like my logs to be grouped under my incoming request. Example: As you can see, all my logs are not grouped under the input request, i've a trace under te request but not under all other logs. This is my example controller: Log lib: To do this with my Google project, I saw that what Google needed to help group everything together was the combination of logback + the spring-google-cloud-trace library, spring-google-cloud-logging, which allowed me to do two things: 1. add a trace/trace ID to each log 2. hang everything on Google Cloud. ### Expected behavior What I expect is that there is a way to allow me to add the trace to the request log and to all logs within this request... Like you see all log under the request have the trace attached.. But with my quarkus app i can't replicate this.. ### Actual behavior _No response_ ### How to Reproduce? quarkusio/quarkus#50321.body: ### Describe the bug Like the title says the problem is into annotation `@RequestParam(required=true)` Into spring you can specify if a request param is required or not and spring ( if required is set ) and it's not passed raise a BadRequestException. But in quarkus i've this problematic point: Required param seems it's not supported each time I added this the app gave me this exception: ```java Caused by: java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Using required @RequestMapping is not supported. Offending method is 'it.test.controller.TestController#customException' at io.quarkus.spring.web.resteasy.reactive.deployment.SpringWebResteasyReactiveProcessor$1.transform(SpringWebResteasyReactiveProcessor.java:312) at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.processor.transformation.AnnotationsTransformer.apply(AnnotationsTransformer.java:74) at org.jboss.jandex.AnnotationOverlayImpl$2.apply(AnnotationOverlayImpl.java:253) at org.jboss.jandex.AnnotationOverlayImpl$2.apply(AnnotationOverlayImpl.java:246) at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap.computeIfAbsent(ConcurrentHashMap.java:1708) at org.jboss.jandex.AnnotationOverlayImpl.getAnnotationsFor(AnnotationOverlayImpl.java:246) at org.jboss.jandex.AnnotationOverlayImpl.annotation(AnnotationOverlayImpl.java:129) at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.processor.transformation.AnnotationStore.getAnnotation(AnnotationStore.java:52) at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.processor.EndpointIndexer.collectEndpoints(EndpointIndexer.java:480) at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.processor.EndpointIndexer.createEndpoints(EndpointIndexer.java:403) at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.processor.EndpointIndexer.createEndpoints(EndpointIndexer.java:312) ... 11 more ``` So i need to validate each request param field manually? Another problem is the default value, if my request param is a boolean, and i don't pass it from the caller, Quarkus set a default value. Why? It's totally wrong.. IE: In this example if you don't pass firstException param Quarkus set it to false.. but does not make sense because the param should be NULL so raise an exception.. ### Expected behavior What I expect is that, since the RequestParam annotation is supported, its functions such as required and raising an exception if a required parameter is not passed are also supported. ### Actual behavior _No response_ ### How to Reproduce? Download my project attached. Run with `./mvnw quarkus:dev` Try to call the endpoint `localhost:8080/test/exception` without passing param `firstException` and can u see the default value problem Try to add `required=true` into the TestController under `@RequestParam` annotation e you can see the error during startup [quarkus-request-param.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22597679/quarkus-request-param.zip) ### Output of `uname -a` or `ver` _No response_ ### Output of `java -version` 21 ### Quarkus version or git rev 3.26.1 ### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`) mvnw quarkus:dev ### Additional information _No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#50322.body:
### Describe the bug

When building a Quarkus app with version 3.27.0 using Maven on Windows, the build fails with java.nio.file.InvalidPathException: UNC path is missing sharename: /\application.yml. This seems to stem from incorrect path handling in ConfigDiagnostic.unknownConfigFiles.


### Expected behavior

Build should succeed and correctly resolve config file paths.

### Actual behavior

Build fails with:
`java.nio.file.InvalidPathException: UNC path is missing sharename: /\application.yml
`

### How to Reproduce?


1. Use Quarkus 3.27.0  on Windows.
2. Place application.yml and profile-specific configs (e.g., application-prod.yml) with default application.yml under the project resources folder /src/main/resources.
3. Run mvn clean install.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.22631.5984]

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "17.0.10" 2024-01-16 OpenJDK Runtime Environment Temurin-17.0.10+7 (build 17.0.10+7) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Temurin-17.0.10+7 (build 17.0.10+7, mixed mode, sharing)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.27.0

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.11 (3e54c93a704957b63ee3494413a2b544fd3d825b) Maven home: C:\ProgramData\chocolatey\lib\maven\apache-maven-3.9.11 Java version: 17.0.10, vendor: Eclipse Adoptium, runtime: C:\Program Files\JavaSemeru\jdk-17.0.10_7 Default locale: en_US, platform encoding: Cp1252 OS name: "windows 11", version: "10.0", arch: "amd64", family: "windows"

### Additional information

### Suspected Code:
`io.quarkus.runtime.configuration.ConfigDiagnostic.unknownConfigFiles(ConfigDiagnostic.java:287)
**Path.of(configFile.getParent().toString(), unprofiledConfigFile)**
`

On Windows, configFile.getParent().toString() returns /, which causes Path.of(...) to fail with an InvalidPathException.

Note: This issue does not occur on Linux. It appears to be related to how paths are constructed and resolved on Windows.
quarkusio/quarkus#50322.title: InvalidPathException on Windows during build: UNC path is missing sharename

quarkusio/quarkus#50336.body:
### Describe the bug

When streaming a ZIP archive assembled on the fly, if an exception occurs during assembly, Quarkus still closes the chunked response by sending the final chunk. As a result, clients such as `wget` or `curl` cannot detect that the archive is incomplete — they treat the download as successful.

### Expected behavior

If an exception occurs while streaming a response, there should be a way to fail the chunked transfer so that the client detects the error.

### Actual behavior

With high-level Quarkus APIs (`StreamingOutput`, `Multi`):
- The response is always closed properly, even on exception.
- Clients cannot distinguish a partial archive from a complete one.

With low-level Vert.x:
- Aborting the connection works as expected.
- But this requires mixing Vert.x core with Mutiny, which may not be reliable.
- A pure Vert.x approach fails with `java.lang.IllegalStateException: Cannot be called on a Vert.x worker thread`

### How to Reproduce?

Please find the reproducer here: https://github.com/Settis/quarkus-chunked-response

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Linux fedora 6.16.7-200.fc42.x86_64

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "21.0.8" 2025-07-15

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.28.1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.9 (Red Hat 3.9.9-14)

### Additional information

Is this behavior expected, or should Quarkus provide a way to fail chunked responses so that clients can reliably detect incomplete downloads?
quarkusio/quarkus#50336.title: Chunked response always finishes cleanly, client cannot detect incomplete download

### Describe the bug

Causing a null pointer exception in a strict Qute template results in a timeout rather than a null pointer exception. It appears to be possible to cause a NPE by referencing the property of a null variable. 

I've noticed that the context shows that the variable is resolved to `Property "size" not found on the base object "null" in expression {bazbaz.size}` but I've not located where the CompletionStage is left hanging in a limbo state rather than being marked as failed when strictRendering is enabled.

TemplateException with the message `Template foo.html [generatedId=1] rendering timeout [10000ms] occured` is thrown instead.

quarkusio/quarkus#50392.body:
### Describe the bug

The https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/48687 introduced this feature.

I wanted to try this but the  `quarkus.extension` , `quarkus.runtime` , `quarkus.application` are not recorded. I would expect that they are recorded on startup of application.

Reproducer and step to reproduce it are described later in this issue.

I play with it little, and it seems when just starting the app the https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/extensions/jfr/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/jfr/runtime/runtime/JfrRuntimeBean.java#L63 is not triggered during the Quarkus run. It's only triggered when Quarkus is stopped, but in that case the `event.shouldCommit()` is `false`.

I was able to able to force the values to be recorded by execute `Recording r = new Recording()` on one endpoint, but this is approach is more like to verify that it work.
Second way I was able to do record value is I set `@Period("1 s")` to https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/extensions/jfr/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/jfr/runtime/runtime/ExtensionEvent.java#L16 and the `quarkus.extension` value was recorded.

Both are probably not great solution, as for these values is enough to be recorded only once at startup.

### Expected behavior

Record `quarkus.extension` , `quarkus.runtime` , `quarkus.application` values at startup when the `quarkus.jfr.runtime.enabled` is true (default value).

### Actual behavior

Not recording `quarkus.extension` , `quarkus.runtime` , `quarkus.application` values at startup even when the `quarkus.jfr.runtime.enabled=true` is set.

### How to Reproduce?

1. `git clone -b jfr-reproducer git@github.com:jedla97/quarkus-reproducers.git`
2. `quarkus dev -Djvm.args="-XX:StartFlightRecording=filename=myrecording.jfr"`
3. `jfr summary myrecording.jfr | grep quarkus.`

the result of jfr summary should be
```
 quarkus.runtime                             0             0
 quarkus.application                         0             0
 quarkus.extension                           0             0
```
4. ``quarkus dev -Djvm.args="-XX:StartFlightRecording=filename=myrecording.jfr"``
5. Visit http://127.0.0.1:8080/dump (start recording to `./dump.jfr`)
6. jfr summary myrecording.jfr | grep quarkus.`

the result of jfr summary should be:
```
 quarkus.extension                          20           376
 quarkus.runtime                             4           140
 quarkus.application                         4           154
 quarkus.Rest                                1           111
 quarkus.RestStart                           0             0
 quarkus.RestEnd                             0             0
```

So the recording working as intended but only when it triggered manually. The unit test for jfr extension starting manually the recording.


### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 21.0.2+13-58)

### Quarkus version or git rev

main, 3.27.0, 3.26.0.CR1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.9 (8e8579a9e76f7d015ee5ec7bfcdc97d260186937)

### Additional information

_No response_

Application with `quarkus-keycloak-admin-rest-client` fails to compile after recent Keycloak bump

This happens after https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/50365

PR adds `runtimeInit.produce(new RuntimeInitializedClassBuildItem("org.keycloak.common.util.SecretGenerator"));` in extensions/keycloak-authorization/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/keycloak/pep/deployment/KeycloakReflectionBuildStep.java

quarkusio/quarkus#50415.body:
### Description

In relation to issue #44046.

In OASFilters registered via @OpenApiFilter annotation, for RUN phase, which additionally have a declared CDI scope like @ApplicationScoped (or even @RequestScoped) should be instantiated by CDI / Arc instead of just registering them as classname on the smallrye openapi library which does a Class.forName. 

Part of it is simple enough since Smallrye's openapi builder allows for registration of an instance of Filter instead of it's classname 

SmallryeOpenApi.Builder via it's addFilter(OASFilter filterInstance) 

One example I was trying to implement was to dinamically filter the openapi spec based on the current user accessing it and his permissions / roles. 

The other part might be a little more tricky since the other issue I faced when trying to implement this is that the request context is not activated when the filter is ran. 


I tried many different approaches for this.

1. Tried @Inject SecurityIdentity in my OASFilter : doesn't work because OASFilter's are not CDI objects but rather instantiated through smallrye openapi library via a Class.forName
2. Tried using CDI.current().select(SecurityIdentity.class) but it also doesn't work because request context is not activated
3. Tried activating Request context programmatically via CDI.current().select(RequestContextController.class).get().activate() and then the previous select. It does work (I do get a securityidentity back) but it is anonymous and not the previously authenticated user...




### Implementation ideas

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50415.title: OASFilter's registered via @OpenApiFilter should be able to be CDI components

quarkusio/quarkus#50427.body: ### Describe the bug I wanted to edit my resource on Windows as I do on Linux, but when I navigate to a DEV UI workspace component, I can see logs instead of a Java file. Windows: But identical application with identical path on Linux looks like this: ### Expected behavior I can use workspace on Windows exactly as I do on Linux ### Actual behavior See the pictures. ### How to Reproduce? On Windows, run: ``` git clone https://github.com/michalvavrik/quarkus-test-suite.git -b feature/fix-devworkspace-on-win cd quarkus-test-suite if not exist "%userprofile%\.m2" mkdir "%userprofile%\.m2" copy .github\quarkus-snapshots-mvn-settings.xml "%userprofile%\.m2\settings.xml" cd http/http-advanced mvn clean verify -Dit.test=DevModeWorkspaceIT -Dreruns=0 ``` If you want to see the screenshot, do: ``` rsync -avz your-user@your-windows-ip:/cygdrive/c/users/your-user/quarkus-test-suite/http/http-advanced/screenshot /tmp/screenshot.png ``` ### Output of `uname -a` or `ver` Windows server 2022 ### Output of `java -version` Temurin 21 ### Quarkus version or git rev 999-SNAPSHOT (probably earlier versions as well, I didn't test it) ### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`) Maven 3.9.9 ### Additional information Firefox

quarkusio/quarkus#50495.body:
### Describe the bug

In a Gradle multi-module project+ Jib using a shared module with `application.properties`:

```properties
quarkus.container-image.name=${quarkus.application.name}
```

one submodule’s quarkus.application.name can leak into another. The result is that one image is built multiple times under the same name, while the other module’s image is never built under its own name.

This started with Quarkus 3.26.3, because properties are being removed in the `EffectiveConfig` task: https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/49824/commits/50e4a237eeaca18a7a94d1080a49ee780e0d8a5f#diff-0eff3ec11780cb64d5e162040ba6cd4b9eb1548151efb8b6f863064dab11aa9fR144.

I rebuilt the `quarkus gradle` application plugin without the 2 lines and the problem disappeared. However it probably causes other issues.

The problematic property being removed  by the `EffectiveConfig` task in this particular case  is `quarkus.package.jar.type=fast-jar`. One workaround is to pass the `fast-jar` as system property to the build ( `-Dquarkus.package.jar.type=fast-jar`). Other is that each module has `quarkus.container-image.name=${quarkus.application.name}` in the corresponding `application properties` file. However there may be other missing properties causing different issues.

### Expected behavior

In a project with 2 modules, 2 distinct module container images are built:

```
Created container image localhost:5000/quarkus-multimodule-repro/module-1:1760043768 (sha256:4e5465b3cc672453b411e5d4fd6c861d0737ef0364f99fa7457454c52a7312be)
Created container image localhost:5000/quarkus-multimodule-repro/module-2:1760043768 (sha256:bd2c2a1d705708d033d1041f62e3ca75171f53db939504181d0b9d17c91df378)
```

### Actual behavior

In a project with 2 modules, module-1 is built x2 with different digest. module-2 is never built.

```
Created container image localhost:5000/quarkus-multimodule-repro/module-1:1760043799 (sha256:42c8d6f7e141788961e9c0632d9246e92cbcffda02ad639e732f550c1433d871)
Created container image localhost:5000/quarkus-multimodule-repro/module-1:1760043799 (sha256:522ba2ed37079791a393bd368aff7ceb5e5e0414b67c59bb5283ee2f9c0bff7b)
```

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/bzprog/quarkus-multimodule-repro

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Linux wblake 6.15.3-arch1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 19 Jun 2025 14:41:19 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "21" 2023-09-19 LTS OpenJDK Runtime Environment Temurin-21+35 (build 21+35-LTS) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Temurin-21+35 (build 21+35-LTS, mixed mode, sharing)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.28.3

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Gradle 9.1.0

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50495.title: Gradle multi-module container image name “spills" across modules after 3.26.3

quarkusio/quarkus#50524.body:
### Description

Running https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/integration-tests/virtual-threads/graphql-virtual-threads/src/test/java/io/quarkus/virtual/graphql/RunOnVirtualThreadIT.java in native mode results in failures:

```
[ERROR] Failures: 
[ERROR]   RunOnVirtualThreadIT>RunOnVirtualThreadTest.testAnnotatedBlockingMutationObject:69 1 expectation failed.
Expected status code <200> but was <404>.

[ERROR]   RunOnVirtualThreadIT>RunOnVirtualThreadTest.testAnnotatedBlockingObject:41 1 expectation failed.
Expected status code <200> but was <404>.
```

you can reproduce it by:

```
cd quarkus/integration-tests/virtual-threads/graphql-virtual-threads
mvn clean verify -Dnative
```

### Implementation ideas

Considering it is an integration test, I'd expect it to pass in the native mode or be explicitly disabled.

quarkusio/quarkus#50526.body:
### Describe the bug

All GraalVM native integration tests fail due to an unexpected version being used to build the native tests. In this case it's a GraalVM CE dev build with labsjdk 25 which has this version output (at 'oracle/graal' master branch):

```
native-image 25 2025-09-16
GraalVM Runtime Environment GraalVM CE 25.1.0-dev+37.1 (build 25+37-jvmci-b06)
Substrate VM GraalVM CE 25.1.0-dev+37.1 (build 25+37, serial gc)
```

Since we are passing a version of `25.1.0` to the `Runtime.Version` class for parsing we fail with:

```
Error:  Caused by: io.smallrye.common.process.AbnormalExitException: Process exited abnormally (pid 3236) with exit code 1 with error output:
Error:   > java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: Invalid version string: '25.1.0'
Error:   > 	at java.base/java.lang.Runtime$Version.parse(Runtime.java:1028)
Error:   > 	at io.quarkus.runtime.graal.GraalVM$Version.<init>(GraalVM.java:136)
Error:   > 	at io.quarkus.runtime.graal.GraalVM$VersionParseHelper.parse(GraalVM.java:63)
Error:   > 	at io.quarkus.runtime.graal.GraalVM$Version.getCurrent(GraalVM.java:241)
```

### Expected behavior

Native integration tests pass on quarkus `main` with `GraalVM 25.1-dev` build.

### Actual behavior

Native integration tests fail on quarkus `main` with `GraalVM 25.1-dev` build. See https://github.com/graalvm/mandrel/actions/runs/18392905506/job/52408369772#step:12:684

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer:

1. Build graal/master from source in the GraalVM CE config
2. Run any quarkus native integration test on the result from step 1.
quarkusio/quarkus#50526.title: [GraalVM next] Allow for future `native-image --version` output parsing

quarkusio/quarkus#50528.body:
### Describe the bug

It looks like that with Quarkus 3.28.3 creating new projects with only extension which are not part of platform always fails.

Here's an example using the Vaadin extension (but can be reproduced also with other extensions, such as `timefold-solver-quarkus`).

```

The same command executed with Quarkus 3.28.2 works fine.

The issue seems related to this change in 3.28.3: #50318

Here's the [link](https://quarkusio.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/187030-users/topic/Create.20project.20failed.20with.203.2E28.2E3.20and.20some.20extensions) to the related discussion on the Quarkus users channel, where suggested workaround is to add also a platform extension (e.g. `rest`) in the command line parameter.

```
mvn -ntp -q -B io.quarkus.platform:quarkus-maven-plugin:create -Dextensions=rest,vaadin -DwithCodestart -DprojectGroupId=com.vaadin.starter -DprojectArtifactId=vaadin-quarkus
```

### Expected behavior

Project should be created even if no platform extensions are specified.

### Actual behavior

The project creation fails with the reported error.

### How to Reproduce?

```
mvn -ntp -q -B io.quarkus.platform:quarkus-maven-plugin:3.28.3:create -Dextensions=vaadin -DwithCodestart -DprojectGrouId=com.vaadin.starter -DprojectArtifactId=vaadin-quarkus
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Linux 6.8.0-85-generic

### Output of `java -version`

Temurin-21.0.6+7

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.28.3

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.11

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50528.title: Project creation with extension not from platform fails with 3.28.3

quarkusio/quarkus#50534.body:
### Describe the bug

When I create a restclient with `@OidcClientFilter` like:
```
@RegisterRestClient
@OidcClientFilter
public interface TokenRestClient {
... whaterver
}
```
and then somewhere else (completely unrelated) in same app, create a class extending the `OidcClientRequestFilter`, like:
```
public class TokenFilter extends OidcClientRequestFilter {
... whatever
}
```

using the rest client will fail with `AmbiguousResolutionException`:
```
java.lang.RuntimeException: Error injecting TokenRestClient 
...
Caused by: jakarta.enterprise.inject.CreationException: Error creating synthetic bean [aKM_yDqHamhcWJrhmLaHd6Za5r4]: jakarta.enterprise.inject.AmbiguousResolutionException: Beans: [CLASS bean [class=TokenFilter, id=Ybf9P7lUK2mqv59eiJWINci54zU], CLASS bean [class=io.quarkus.oidc.client.filter.OidcClientRequestFilter, id=gaEtyHdCXrr9_9MgSsDOprS4FCI]]
...
```
Making the rest client unusable. In my case `TokenRestClient` and `TokenFilter` are in not related to each other anyhow, even using different OIDC_CLIENT. But still mere presence of class extending `OidcClientRequestFilter` causes rest client with `@OidcClientFilter` to fail.

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

_No response_

openjdk 17.0.13 2024-10-15

999-SNAPSHOT

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#50538.body: ### Describe the bug According to the [documentation](https://quarkus.io/guides/telemetry-micrometer#metrics-for-netty-memory-management) the Netty metric `allocator.memory.used` (`netty_allocator_memory_used` in Prometheus) should report the "amount of memory currently used by the allocator". However, for the _heap_ `memory_type` of the _UnpooledByteBufAllocator_ `allocator_type` we are seeing ever increasing values, which at some point in time exceed both the maximum JVM heap (1GB in the example) and the set container limits (1.5 GB) by far. Therefore either the metric tracks something different or it's not tracking correctly. See also https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/45115#issuecomment-3397893177 for a first guess. ### Expected behavior The metric should report a value that is within the JVM heap size and not every increasing. ### Actual behavior The metric value constantly increases beyond any memory limits. ### How to Reproduce? Run an application that uses netty and monitor the metric `netty_allocator_memory_used{allocator_type="UnpooledByteBufAllocator", memory_type="heap", name="unpooled"}` over several hours. ### Output of `uname -a` or `ver` inux xxx-5dc96f57b4-545ms 6.8.0-1039-aws #41~22.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Thu Sep 11 10:54:48 UTC 2025 x86_64 GNU/Linux ### Output of `java -version` OpenJDK Runtime Environment Temurin-21.0.8+9 (build 21.0.8+9-LTS) ### Quarkus version or git rev 3.20.2 ### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`) Apache Maven 3.9.11 ### Additional information _No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50538.title: Netty metric allocator.memory.used reports unreasonable value

quarkusio/quarkus#50571.body:
### Describe the bug

We have a multimodule project, and one of the modules is a quarkus extension.
The structure is similar to the following (many modules are omitted for brevity)

```
├── vaadin
├── vaadin-bom
├── vaadin-core
├── vaadin-gradle-plugin
├── vaadin-platform-test
├── vaadin-prod-bundle
├── vaadin-quarkus
│   ├── vaadin-quarkus-extension
│   └── vaadin-quarkus-extension-deployment
```

In the root POM file, some modules are pointing at POM files instead of directories, like in the following snippet.

```xml
<modules>
    ...
    <module>vaadin-gradle-plugin/pom-portal.xml</module>
    ...    
</modules>        
```

With Quarkus 3.27 (we use the latest LTS version), the build of `vaadin-quarkus-extension` modules fails with the following errors.

```
[ERROR] Failed to execute goal io.quarkus:quarkus-extension-maven-plugin:3.27.0:extension-descriptor (default) on project vaadin-quarkus-extension: Execution default of goal io.quarkus:quarkus-extension-maven-plugin:3.27.0:extension-descriptor failed: The following errors were encountered while processing Quarkus application dependencies:
[ERROR] 1) Failed to load POM from /xxxxx/vaadin-platform-test/pom-dev.xml/pom.xml
[ERROR]   at io.quarkus.bootstrap.resolver.maven.workspace.WorkspaceLoader.readModel(WorkspaceLoader.java:63)
[ERROR]   ...
[ERROR] 2) Failed to load POM from /xxxxx/vaadin-gradle-plugin/pom-portal.xml/pom.xml
[ERROR]   at io.quarkus.bootstrap.resolver.maven.workspace.WorkspaceLoader.readModel(WorkspaceLoader.java:63)
[ERROR]   ...
[ERROR] 3) Failed to load POM from /xxxxx/vaadin-prod-bundle/pom-unoptimized.xml/pom.xml
[ERROR]   at io.quarkus.bootstrap.resolver.maven.workspace.WorkspaceLoader.readModel(WorkspaceLoader.java:63)
[ERROR]   ...
```

The problem seems to be that Quarkus always expects `<module>` entries to reference a directory, so it tries to find a POM file in a wrong location (e.g. `/xxxxx/vaadin-prod-bundle/pom-unoptimized.xml/pom.xml`).

Looking at the Maven documentation (https://maven.apache.org/pom.html, Aggregation (or Multi-Module)
 section) it seems that using POM files with `<module>` is a valid configuration.
 
 > A pom packaged project may aggregate the build of a set of projects by listing them as modules, which are relative paths to the directories or the POM files of those projects.

So, I wonder if this could be a bug in the Quarkus workspace loader.

To be noted that the error does not occur with Quarkus 3.20.

### Expected behavior

Quarkus maven extension plugin should not fail to load the workspace when POM files are used to setup submodules in Maven projects.

### Actual behavior

The plugin fails because it tries to resolve a wrong path for submodule POM.

### How to Reproduce?

Create a multi-module maven project and make at least one of the `<module>` entries point to a POM file instead of a directory.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Linux  6.8.0-85-generic

### Output of `java -version`

Temurin-21.0.6+7

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.27.0

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Maven 3.9.11

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50571.title: WorkspaceLoader fails if a Maven module tag references a pom file instead of a directory

quarkusio/quarkus#50572.body:
### Describe the bug

If I want to define a global Hibernate **@FilterDef** the best practive way is to use a **package-info.java** next to the entities.

But this package-info.java during tests causes a WARN:

`2025-10-15 10:38:52,125 WARN  [io.qua.dep.ind.IndexWrapper] (build-26) Failed to index org.acme: Class does not exist in ClassLoader QuarkusClassLoader:Deployment Class Loader: TEST for JUnitQuarkusTest-no-profile (QuarkusTest)@7446d8d5`


_HibernateOrmProcessor.generateProxies_ calls the _IndexWrapper.getClassByName_ with the packageName and not an actual className as expected this causes the mentioned warn.

Everything seems to work fine expect the warning so, it probably would be enough to somewhere in HibernateORMProcessor filter out packages from the list of classes.

### Expected behavior

Test should run without producing an WARN message each time.

### Actual behavior

`2025-10-15 10:38:52,125 WARN  [io.qua.dep.ind.IndexWrapper] (build-26) Failed to index org.acme: Class does not exist in ClassLoader QuarkusClassLoader:Deployment Class Loader: TEST for JUnitQuarkusTest-no-profile (QuarkusTest)@7446d8d5`

Message is shown during test runs if package-info.java is present with hibernate @FilderDef inside.

### How to Reproduce?

quarkusio/quarkus#50574.body:
### Describe the bug

While investigating https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/50469 we noticed that Netty fails to detect whether unaligned accesses are supported in native executables and [falls back to pattern matching the architecture](https://github.com/netty/netty/blob/b81050de47fb60d89eda1f639d2def77943f67c3/common/src/main/java/io/netty/util/internal/PlatformDependent0.java#L451).

Since the pattern doesn't include `AArch64` this causes accesses on `AArch64` to be unaligned.

### Expected behavior

DEBUG logs should contain:

```
java.nio.Bits.unaligned: available, true
```

### Actual behavior

DEBUG logs on `AArch64` contain:

```
java.nio.Bits.unaligned: unavailable, false, Unable to make static boolean java.nio.Bits.unaligned() accessible: module java.base does not "opens java.nio" to unnamed module @7a360554
```

DEBUG logs on `x86_64` contain:

```
java.nio.Bits.unaligned: unavailable, true, Unable to make static boolean java.nio.Bits.unaligned() accessible: module java.base does not "opens java.nio" to unnamed module @7a360554
```


### How to Reproduce?

```
wget "https://code.quarkus.io/d?cn=code.quarkus.io" -O code.zip
unzip code.zip
cd code-with-quarkus
./mvnw -Dnative package
./target/code-with-quarkus-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT-runner -Dquarkus.log.level=DEBUG
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Mandrel or GraalVM version (if different from Java)

Both Mandrel 23.1 and 25 

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.28.3

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

Should be fixed by backporting https://github.com/netty/netty/pull/15752 to Netty 4.1
quarkusio/quarkus#50574.title: `java.nio.Bits.unaligned`: unavailable in Netty resulting in Netty unaligned accesses not being enabled on AArch64

quarkusio/quarkus#50617.body:
### Describe the bug

With the following scenario:

- Library `libTest` defines a methods for loading classes (basically a wrapper for `Class.forName` and `Thread.currentThread().getContextClassLoader().loadClass`
- The test in an application uses this library to load a class defined in the test sources

Previously this also did not work when using `Class.forName` in the library -- it fails with a `ClassNotFoundException`, howerver, using the class loader of the current thread used to work.

Sadly this seems to break when running with JDK 25. Specifically when a test spawns a new thread and then calls the library function for loadling the class in the new thread. Here, a class is found, however, when creating a new instance of the object, it is incompatible with the remaining code due to a classloader mismatch apparently.

---

See [mensinda/quarkus-stuff](https://github.com/mensinda/quarkus-stuff/tree/classLoadingError) for the specific implementation.


### Expected behavior

The test also passes with JDK 25

### Actual behavior

```
java.util.concurrent.ExecutionException: java.lang.ClassCastException: class test.TestImpl cannot be cast to class libtest.BaseClass (test.TestImpl is in unnamed module of loader 'app'; libtest.BaseClass is in unnamed module of loader io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader @41709512)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.wrapInExecutionException(CompletableFuture.java:345)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.reportGet(CompletableFuture.java:440)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture.get(CompletableFuture.java:2094)
        at test.LoadingTest.loadingInThreadWithCurrentThreadInLib(LoadingTest.java:111)
        at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:565)
        at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.runExtensionMethod(QuarkusTestExtension.java:998)
        at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.interceptTestMethod(QuarkusTestExtension.java:846)
        at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1604)
        at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1604)
Caused by: java.lang.ClassCastException: class test.TestImpl cannot be cast to class libtest.BaseClass (test.TestImpl is in unnamed module of loader 'app'; libtest.BaseClass is in unnamed module of loader io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader @41709512)
        at test.LoadingTest.inThreadImplCurrentThread(LoadingTest.java:99)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture$AsyncRun.run(CompletableFuture.java:1825)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.CompletableFuture$AsyncRun.exec(CompletableFuture.java:1817)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinTask.doExec(ForkJoinTask.java:511)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool$WorkQueue.topLevelExec(ForkJoinPool.java:1450)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinPool.runWorker(ForkJoinPool.java:2019)
        at java.base/java.util.concurrent.ForkJoinWorkerThread.run(ForkJoinWorkerThread.java:187)
```

### How to Reproduce?

Install both JDK 21 and 25 (and maybe other versions)

[Setup a `toolchains.xml`](https://maven.apache.org/guides/mini/guide-using-toolchains.html)

```bash
git clone git@github.com:mensinda/quarkus-stuff.git
cd quarkus-stuff
git switch classLoadingError

./run.sh 21 # All non-disabled tests pass
./run.sh 25 # One test fails
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Linux XXXXX 6.14.0-33-generic #33~24.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Sep 19 17:02:30 UTC 2 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "25" 2025-09-16

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.28.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.11 (3e54c93a704957b63ee3494413a2b544fd3d825b)

### Additional information

_No response_

Originally reported for Camel Quarkus here:

https://github.com/apache/camel-quarkus/issues/7843

There's a reproducer project here (run with `mvn clean quarkus:dev`):

https://github.com/jam01/camel-log-repro/tree/main

When an exception is thrown from within Camel route processing, Camel will include some extended information about the message exchange in the generated log message.

Camel wraps the message exchange body within the log message between `{}` (not entirely sure why TBH). This seems to break JBoss logging formatting.

E.g if the message body is a string like `foo`, then you get a log message string like:

```
Message History (source location is disabled)
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Source                                   ID                             Processor                                          Elapsed (ms)
                                         route1/route1                  from[timer://foo?period=1s]                                  12
                                         route1/setBody1                setBody[constant{foo}]                                        2
```

quarkusio/quarkus#50625.body:
### Describe the bug

I'm currently looking into a graceful shutdown for Keycloak, which uses Quarkus as its foundation. 

When gracefully shutting down Quarkus, the `GracefulShutdownFilter` shows a 503 error message to users and calling services, which could be a more graceful shutdown IMHO. 

https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/cb7338252704063bba9312703ffc951a74803b52/extensions/vertx-http/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/http/runtime/filters/GracefulShutdownFilter.java#L43-L47

In addition to that, there is no handling for HTTP/2 connections which should receive a "go away" instead of a connection close header.

At least in the context of Keycloak, I would rather have the request handled than returning a 503 - especially as any retry to the same server would lead to another 503.

### Expected behavior

* During graceful shutdown, all requests (including new ones) are handled normally the number of currently processed requests reaches zero
* For HTTP/1.1 connections, they receive a "Connection: Close" 
* For HTTP/2 connections, they receive a go-away either on the next request, or the moment the shutdown is invoked
* Quarkus should stop accepting new connections



### Actual behavior

* Once the shutdown is in progress, newly arriving requests receive a 503 immediately, leading to problems with calling services and users operating a browser
* HTTP/2 connections receive a connection header which is forbidden: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Reference/Headers/Connection
* Quarkus continues to accept new connections


### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer: https://github.com/ahus1/quarkus-graceful-shutdown

1. build the app: `./mvnw package`
2. run the app: `java -jar target/quarkus-app/quarkus-run.jar`
3. in a new terminal, call the slow endpoint: `curl -v localhost:8080`
4. initiate the shutdown of the app with Ctrl-C
5. in a new terminal, call the slow endpoint: `curl -v localhost:8080`

The last step shows 503 where it should IMHO show a 200 as it is more user and service friendly

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

OpenJDK Runtime Environment (Red_Hat-21.0.8.0.9-1) (build 21.0.8+9)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.27.0

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.11 

### Additional information

I can try to prepare a PR once there is some feedback if handling new requests is an option.
quarkusio/quarkus#50625.title: Quarkus graceful shutdown returns 503 during graceful shutdown

quarkusio/quarkus#50652.body:
### Describe the bug

When running a Quarkus application that uses a REST client configured with
the property
`quarkus.rest-client."<client>".disable-default-mapper=true` defined in `application.properties`, the configuration works correctly in JVM mode and the default JSON mapper is disabled and the REST client correctly handles responses such as HTTP 404 without mapping errors.

However, after compiling to a native image, the same configuration appears to be ignored. The REST client continues to use the default mapper, resulting in mapping exceptions.

### Expected behavior

The `disable-default-mapper` property should disable the default JSON mapper, resulting in a simple status-based response (no mapping errors), consistent with JVM mode behavior.

### Actual behavior

The native executable still applies the default JSON mapper, producing mapping errors such as:

```plain
HTTP Request to /notfound failed, error id: a68f31c7-0ed9-449f-bec0-d3134c416dab-1: org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.ClientWebApplicationException: Received: 'Not Found, status code 404' when invoking REST Client method: 'com.sample.rest.GoogleRestClient#notFound'
        ...
Caused by: jakarta.ws.rs.WebApplicationException: Not Found, status code 404
        at io.quarkus.rest.client.reactive.runtime.DefaultMicroprofileRestClientExceptionMapper.toThrowable(DefaultMicroprofileRestClientExceptionMapper.java:19)
        ...
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Define the following in `application.properties`:

   ```properties
   quarkus.rest-client.nodefaultmapper.disable-default-mapper=true
   ```

2. Build a native executable:

   ```bash
   ./gradlew build -Dquarkus.native.enabled=true -Dquarkus.package.jar.enabled=false
   ```

3. Run the native binary:

   ```bash
   ./build/disable-default-mapper-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT-runner
   ```

4. Call the endpoint:

   ```bash
   curl localhost:8080/notfound
   ```

### Minimal Reproducer

**GoogleRestClient.java**

```java
@RegisterRestClient(configKey = "nodefaultmapper", baseUri = "https://www.google.com")
public interface GoogleRestClient {
    @GET @Path("/") Uni<Response> found();
    @GET @Path("/doesnotexists") Uni<Response> notFound();
}
```

**ResponseCheckerResource.java**

```java
@Path("/")
public class ResponseCheckerResource {
    @Inject @RestClient GoogleRestClient googleRestClient;

    @GET @Path("/notfound") @Produces(MediaType.TEXT_PLAIN)
    public Uni<String> notFound() {
        return googleRestClient.notFound()
            .onItem().transform(r -> r.getStatusInfo().getReasonPhrase());
    }
}
```

**application.properties**

```properties
quarkus.rest-client.nodefaultmapper.disable-default-mapper=true
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Darwin Kernel Version 25.0.0

### Output of `java -version`

OpenJDK Runtime Environment Temurin-21.0.6+7 (build 21.0.6+7-LTS)

### Mandrel or GraalVM version (if different from Java)

Oracle GraalVM 21.0.3+7.1 (Java 21.0.3+7-LTS)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.28.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Gradle 9.1.0

### Additional information

If the property is set via an **environment variable** instead of in `application.properties`, the native executable behaves as expected:

```bash
QUARKUS_REST_CLIENT_NODEFAULTMAPPER_DISABLE_DEFAULT_MAPPER=true ./build/disable-default-mapper-1.0.0-SNAPSHOT-runner
```

This correctly disables the default mapper:

```plain
$> curl localhost:8080/notfound
Not Found
```

It appears that configuration properties defined in `application.properties` for disabling the default mapper may not be properly registered or propagated during native image build time.
In contrast, environment variables are correctly read at runtime, which explains why the workaround functions.
quarkusio/quarkus#50652.title: `quarkus.rest-client."client".disable-default-mapper` Ignored in Native Mode

quarkusio/quarkus#50703.body:
### Description

## Expected Behavior

[RFC 9126](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9126) introduces pushed authorization requests (PAR) for OAuth. In essence, pushed authorization requests allow the client to send authorization request information to the authorization server through back channel communication rather than through the user agent. Not exposing the authorization request data to the user agent makes it harder for an attacker to manipulate said info, e.g. change the requested scopes or tamper with any of the additional query parameters defined in one of the many OAuth enhancement RFCs. Once the client has successfully pushed the authorization request data, the AS responds with a unique identifier that the client must then send as a query parameter during a normal authorization request. From there everything works as usual.

PAR are part of the upcoming [FAPI 2.0](https://openid.net/specs/fapi-2_0-baseline-01.html) and more and more AS/OP products and frameworks such as Keycloak, Spring Security, Forgerock and Nimbus OAuth are adding support for PAR. It would be great if the Quarkus OIDC extension would support PAR out of the box. PAR endpoints can be discovered from the AS/OP published metadata. Ideally, it would be as simple as setting a boolean flag in the client configuration to configure the client to use PAR.

(.NET 9 has adopted discovery: if the IDP supports PAR, then it is used by default.)

## Current Behavior

PAR not supported in the Quarkus OIDC extension.

## Context

Any and all information being transported through the user agent during an authorization request can be manipulated. JWT-Secured Authorization Request (JAR) address this but can lead to quite long query strings which can cause problems with some AS or the web server on which they are hosted. Signed and encrypted JWTs are required to add tamper proofing and confidentiality, respectively. This adds more complexity on the client side. PAR finds a middle ground as it adds a good degree of confidentiality and tamper proofing by not passing the sensitive authorization data through the user agent. For added security, PAR can be combined with JAR to alleviate the long request string problem.

## Additional references

Here is [a presentation of the Authorization Code Flow with Pushed Authorization Requests](https://auth0.com/docs/get-started/authentication-and-authorization-flow/authorization-code-flow/authorization-code-flow-with-par), that can be compared with [the usual Authorization Code Flow](https://auth0.com/docs/get-started/authentication-and-authorization-flow/authorization-code-flow).

(Disclaimer: this issue description was largely copy-pasted from [the issue requesting support in Spring Security](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-security/issues/11301).)

### Implementation ideas

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50703.title: Support for Pushed Authorization Requests (PAR) in OIDC

quarkusio/quarkus#50707.body:
### Describe the bug

When using custom Jose4J validators with OIDC bearer token authentication, the `WWW-Authenticate `response header doesn't include the step-up authentication challenge details (error, error_description, acr_values) as it does when using `@AuthenticationContext` annotation.

### Expected behavior

The response should contain the full step-up authentication challenge:

`WWW-Authenticate: Bearer error="insufficient_user_authentication", error_description="Token missing ACR claim", acr_values="gold,platinum"`

### Actual behavior

When a custom Jose4J validator throws an AuthenticationFailedException with ACR values:
`throw new AuthenticationFailedException(
    "Token missing ACR claim", 
    Map.of(OidcConstants.ACR_VALUES, "gold,platinum")
);`

The response only contains:

`WWW-Authenticate: Bearer`

### How to Reproduce?

You can go through our quarkus-test-suite repo in my branch here: https://github.com/quarkus-qe/quarkus-test-suite/compare/main...jcarranzan:quarkus-test-suite:feat/oidc-step-up-auth

And enable the test disabled: `testCustomJose4jValidatorRequiresMultipleAcr`

https://github.com/jcarranzan/quarkus-test-suite/blob/4aeb91dc000d95f0adf5270059787656d9fa3739/security/keycloak-oidc-client-extended/src/test/java/io/quarkus/ts/security/keycloak/oidcclient/extended/restclient/AbstractOidcStepUpAuthenticationIT.java#L215

Then you will see the error:
```
[ERROR] io.quarkus.ts.security.keycloak.oidcclient.extended.restclient.OidcStepUpAuthenticationIT.testCustomJose4jValidatorRequiresMultipleAcr -- Time elapsed: 0.187 s <<< FAILURE!
java.lang.AssertionError: 
1 expectation failed.
Expected header "WWW-Authenticate" was not (a string containing "insufficient_user_authentication" and a string containing "acr_values" and a string containing "gold" and a string containing "platinum"), was "Bearer". Headers are:
www-authenticate=Bearer
content-length=0
```

 

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

_No response_

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50707.title: Custom Jose4J validators don't propagate step-up authentication challenge details to WWW-Authenticate header

quarkusio/quarkus#50720.body:
### Description

In general, the `@AuthorizationPolicy` annotation https://quarkus.io/guides/security-authorize-web-endpoints-reference#custom-http-security-policy provides advantage over the permission checkers only in specific situations. Nevertheless, it can be more valuable for WebSockets Next than for Jakarta REST endpoints. Here are reasons why I think we should start supporting it:

- We should be able to match the `@AuthorizationPolicy` annotation with the WS Next endpoints even with enabled proactive authentication and run it as part of the authorization handler, before WS Next starts processing
- In addition to the general security check, the authorization policy also allows to augment identity if it is convenient to combine check and augmentation
- Easier access to `RoutingContext` in case incoming HTTP request needs to be checked
- We support it for Jakarta REST and we also support global `HttpSecurityPolicy` for all the routes, there is a good chance someone already is using such policy and would like to reduce it to certain endpoints using the annotation

I think this task will require refactoring, but not so much adding a new processing (I mean, we shouldn't pay a price to support this).


### Implementation ideas

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50720.title: Support `@AuthorizationPolicy` annotation on WebSockets Next endpoints

quarkusio/quarkus#50740.body:
### Describe the bug

We have a large codebase based on Quarkus which we test with complex QuarkusTests. And since the move to Quarkus 3.27 we start seing random failures of their run reporting a stacktrace like:

```
java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to start quarkus
  at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.throwBootFailureException(QuarkusTestExtension.java:666)
  at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.interceptTestClassConstructor(QuarkusTestExtension.java:761)

  at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:580)
  at io.quarkus.runner.bootstrap.StartupActionImpl.run(StartupActionImpl.java:350)
  at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.doJavaStart(QuarkusTestExtension.java:232)
  at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.ensureStarted(QuarkusTestExtension.java:640)
  at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.beforeAll(QuarkusTestExtension.java:685)
  ... 1 more
Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "io.quarkus.arc.ArcContainer.getCurrentContextFactory()" because the return value of "io.quarkus.arc.Arc.container()" is null
  at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder.getIgnoredArcContextKeysSupplier(VertxCoreRecorder.java:605)
  at io.quarkus.runner.recorded.VertxCoreProcessor$dontPropagateCdiContext731300267.deploy_0(Unknown Source)
  at io.quarkus.runner.recorded.VertxCoreProcessor$dontPropagateCdiContext731300267.deploy(Unknown Source)

```

### Expected behavior

NullPointerException should not occur during the test run

Hello,
I have a QuarkusTest in my project which uses Kafka and the KafkaCompanionResource. At the moment I use the quarkus version 3.28.5 and get the following error if I start the test:

```

The problem is that at some point I get double quotes inside this name. If I use a test resource like this:

quarkusio/quarkus#50754.body:
### Describe the bug

Reviewing and doing a small regression test development coverage for issue #50336 , I've noticed that the fix in [PR #50361 ](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/50361) only addressed reactive streaming (`Multi`/`Publisher`) but did not cover the blocking `StreamingOutput` API.


### Expected behavior

Similar to the fix (PR #50361 )for reactive streams (`Multi`), when an exception occurs during `StreamingOutput` streaming:
- The server should call `HttpServerResponse.reset()` 
- The final zero-length chunk should NOT be sent
- HTTP clients should be able to detect the connection was abruptly closed

### Actual behavior

When using `StreamingOutput` with `@Blocking`, if an exception occurs during streaming:
- The chunked response is closed normally
- HTTP clients cannot detect the incomplete transfer

### Description

Coming from this discussion: https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/discussions/50756. We realized the `hibernate-spatial` extension for Hibernate ORM (see its [documentation](https://docs.hibernate.org/orm/7.1/userguide/html_single/#spatial)) is not included in Qarkus' BOM.

We should be able to write an integration test against PostGIS, the PostgreSQL extension for handling spatial information, relatively easily. Once we have that, we can add the required `hibernate-spatial` dependency to the ones we already offer dependency management.

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#50769.body:
### Describe the bug

We are running into a bit of a weird bug in Quarkus. I admit that I don't fully understand why this bug is happening yet, but I'm a bit stuck so I thought I'd open a bug report and maybe the Quarkus team and my team can try to understand it together.

We recently started to use the Quarkus docker compose dev service to run some integration tests (basically our whole internal system is spun up as a docker compose setup).  This works locally, but when I then try to run it in the CI, where we know our docker setup works because normal Quarkus dev services that use testcontainers work perfectly fine, I get:
> Build step io.quarkus.devservices.deployment.compose.ComposeDevServicesProcessor#config threw an exception: java.lang.IllegalStateException: No container runtime was found. Make sure you have either Docker or Podman installed in your environment.

Now if you actually grab a few lines in the log, it becomes quite interesting:
```
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,877 INFO  [org.tes.doc.DockerClientProviderStrategy] (build-3) Found Docker environment with local Unix socket (unix:///var/run/docker.sock)
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,912 INFO  [org.tes.DockerClientFactory] (build-3) Docker host IP address is 172.17.0.1
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,950 INFO  [org.tes.DockerClientFactory] (build-3) Connected to docker: 
      Server Version: 27.3.1
      API Version: 1.47
      Operating System: Flatcar Container Linux by Kinvolk 4230.2.4 (Oklo)
      Total Memory: 7941 MB
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,969 INFO  [org.tes.ima.PullPolicy] (build-3) Image pull policy will be performed by: DefaultPullPolicy()
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,972 INFO  [org.tes.uti.ImageNameSubstitutor] (build-3) Image name substitution will be performed by: DefaultImageNameSubstitutor (composite of 'ConfigurationFileImageNameSubstitutor' and 'PrefixingImageNameSubstitutor')
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,980 INFO  [org.tes.DockerClientFactory] (build-3) Checking the system...
    2025-10-29 15:08:26,981 INFO  [org.tes.DockerClientFactory] (build-3) ✔︎ Docker server version should be at least 1.6.0
Gradle Test Executor 8 STANDARD_ERROR
    java.lang.RuntimeException: io.quarkus.builder.BuildException: Build failure: Build failed due to errors
    	[error]: Build step io.quarkus.devservices.deployment.compose.ComposeDevServicesProcessor#config threw an exception: java.lang.IllegalStateException: No container runtime was found. Make sure you have either Docker or Podman installed in your environment.
```

Clearly Quarkus can find a working docker environment, because a few log lines earlier it literally says `Found Docker environment with local Unix socket (unix:///var/run/docker.sock)`, but the Docker compose docker detection seems  to be failing. And I am really struggling to understand why, because this locally works, and our CI docker setup is not that different from locally running this (it even mounts /var/lib/docker.sock rather than using a TCP endpoint).

Quarkus Docker Compose works fine locally, and other Quarkus docker stuff works fine in our CI, it is purely the Quarkus docker compose that fails in the CI specifically.

### Expected behavior

Quarkus docker compose should be able to detect Docker in the same scenarios as othe Quarkus docker-dependent subsystems

### Actual behavior

The docker runtime is specifically not found in our CI by the Quarkus Docker compose dev services, even though other dev services can find our docker runtime perfectly fine.

### How to Reproduce?

CI Job that fails: https://gitlab.com/rug-digitallab/products/themis/themis-core/-/jobs/11896925277

How to reproduce:
1. Fork https://gitlab.com/rug-digitallab/products/themis/themis-core
2. Look at `ls/256-integration-tests-for-quarkus-devs`, which is a modified version of the problem branch `ls/256-integration-tests` such that you can run the CI in a fork on the GitLab shared runners, since the integration tests would normally run on a private runner of ours.
3. The CI job is expected to fail on GitLab shared runners (there's a reason we have it on our private runners), but it should fail longer after the spin-up of docker compose, so you should still be able to use it to debug.
4. The unit-test job will contain the failures. Note that this job actually also tests other microservices that use normal dev services rather than the compose dev service, and they work perfectly fine in the same job, reinforcing the fact that it's something specific to the compose dev service.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Container image of the CI: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/openjdk-21:1.23-6.1761164967

### Output of `java -version`

OpenJDK 21.0.8

### Quarkus version or git rev

Quarkus 3.28.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Gradle 9.1

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50769.title: Docker runtime not detected for Compose dev services

quarkusio/quarkus#50801.body:
### Description

OIDC request and response filters should be asynchronous, otherwise they can block. It has not happened yet as they are typically used for fast request specific checks/updates, but it will happen sooner or later

### Implementation ideas

Since other OIDC public API providers like `TenantConfigResolver` that OIDC users work with are async, I believe it is OK to deprecate current sync methods and introduce Uni ones

quarkusio/quarkus#50803.body:
### Description

This issue is a newer version of #11392.

Quarkus applications should be able to run scheduled tasks in a `Run As` mode, where a user, and optionally, roles, are assigned to a scheduled task. 

Switching the user identity for the current HTTP request is out of scope but can be reviewed later.

### Implementation ideas

Currently there are two proposals.

1. Add attributes to the `@Scheduled` annotation as suggested at #11392, for example, `@Scheduled(cron="0 30 2 * * ?", user="scheduler", role="user")` 
2. Introduce an annotation like `@RunAsUser`, for example, `@RunAsUser(user="scheduler", role="user")`  and restrict it be effective only when it is used as part of the `@Scheduled` call or possibly some blocking HTTP request call.

IMHO, option 1 is simpler, it makes it clearly constrained by `@Scheduled`. Supporting various flows (scheduled or current HTTP request ones) with a single annotation like `@RunAsUser` can cause ambiguities. If we ever decide to allow to apply the `Run As` concept to the current HTTP request then we can introduce Quarkus specific `RunAs`, like `RunAsUser` or similar to JBoss's `RunAsPrincipal` that will take both a name and roles and some other attributes.

CC @michalvavrik @mkouba @wjglerum
quarkusio/quarkus#50803.title: Allow to assign a user and roles to a scheduled task
