quarkusio/quarkus#50847.body:
### Description

The current logging pattern that Quarkus is using by default is shortening package names and other logger category identifiers, such as explicit names.
This was meant to improve readability of logs - however this makes it sometimes difficult to actually figure out what the full name of the category is; in some cases we're also falling short in making sure that the user is aware that they are reading a shortened version.

For example, as I was updating an old reference application which we use to test bootstrap efficiency, I was now being flooded by deprecation warnings from Hibernate ORM:
```
2025-11-04 13:54:34,948 WARN  [org.hib.orm.deprecation] (Quarkus Main Thread) HHH90000033: Encountered use of deprecated annotation [interface jakarta.persistence.Temporal] at nl.topicus.eduarte.model.entities.participatie.olc.OlcWaarneming.eindTijd.
2025-11-04 13:54:34,948 WARN  [org.hib.orm.deprecation] (Quarkus Main Thread) HHH90000033: Encountered use of deprecated annotation [interface jakarta.persistence.Temporal] at nl.topicus.eduarte.model.entities.participatie.olc.OlcWaarneming.datum.
```

Reconfiguring the application to set the following would NOT be effective:
```
quarkus.log.category."org.hib.orm.deprecation".level=OFF
```

The problem is that the actual category name is `org.hibernate.orm.deprecation` - but it got shortened automatically.
I happend to know how to expand it, but I see it as a problem for the cases in which people don't actually know how to expand the cathegory name - this could be fairly hard to accomplish when having many dependancies and not having the right clues of where it's coming from. I also think some people might not be aware at all that the category name had been shortened.

Dicussed this further with the team:
 - [#dev > Usability puzzle for "package shortening" in logger output](https://quarkusio.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/187038-dev/topic/Usability.20puzzle.20for.20.22package.20shortening.22.20in.20logger.20output/with/553664163)

### Implementation ideas

We got to the conclusion that the safest approach is to not shorten cathegories by default.

```
-         @WithDefault("%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss,SSS} %-5p [%c{3.}] (%t) %s%e%n")
+        @WithDefault("%d{yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss,SSS} %-5p [%c] (%t) %s%e%n")
```
quarkusio/quarkus#50847.title: Avoid shortening logger categories identifiers by default

quarkusio/quarkus#50876.body:
### Describe the bug

In Dev-Mode, Quarkus randomly shows 404 instead of the Error 500 page in case of deployment error.

### Expected behavior

Always show the 500 error

### Actual behavior

Randomly shows 404 when requesting in burst (already experienced in browser)
It seems to be when a request arrive while the previous one is processed
Also it seems to processing is very slow as it show the loader while it should be instant

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/ia3andy/reproducer-resource-not-found.git

Clone the repo
Start dev mode
Run GreetingResourceTest.testHelloEndpoint (it could fail after a few run)

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.29

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#50876.title: Dev-Mode - 404 instead of the Dev Error page (500) on deployment error

quarkusio/quarkus#50886.body:
### Describe the bug

Calling `ServerCall.close()` in a gRPC `ServerInterceptor` (e.g., to implement an authentication interceptor) is a [supported pattern by upstream gRPC](https://github.com/grpc/grpc-java/blob/v1.76.0/examples/example-jwt-auth/src/main/java/io/grpc/examples/jwtauth/JwtServerInterceptor.java) and works properly in Quarkus by default. However, when using the Vert.x gRPC implementation by setting `quarkus.grpc.server.use-separate-server=false`, calling an RPC that is protected by an interceptor implementing this pattern results in `java.lang.IllegalStateException: Already closed` on the server, though the correct status is still returned to the client.

### Expected behavior

`ServerInterceptor`s that call `ServerCall.close()` and don't cause `Already closed` errors with the default Quarkus configuration do not cause `Already closed` errors when `quarkus.grpc.server.use-separate-server=false`.

### Actual behavior

`ServerInterceptor`s that call `ServerCall.close()` and work properly in the default Quarkus configuration result in `IllegalStateException: Already closed` errors when `quarkus.grpc.server.use-separate-server=false`.

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer GitHub repository: https://github.com/lberrymage/quarkus-grpc-interceptor-close-issue-reproducer
Reproducer ZIP: [reproducer-main.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23379261/reproducer-main.zip)

Reproduction steps:

1. [Install `grpcurl`](https://github.com/fullstorydev/grpcurl/blob/v1.9.3/README.md#installation).
1. Run `./mvnw quarkus:dev`.
1. Run `grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8080 acme.ping.PingService/Ping`.
1. Observe server logs to see `java.lang.IllegalStateException: Already closed`.
1. Quit the Quarkus process.
1. Comment out the only config line in `src/main/resources/application.properties`.
1. Run `./mvnw quarkus:dev`.
1. Run `grpcurl -plaintext localhost:9000 acme.ping.PingService/Ping`.
1. Observe server logs to see no exceptions.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

```
Linux hostname 6.17.6-arch1-1 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed, 29 Oct 2025 22:23:47 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

### Output of `java -version`

```
openjdk version "25" 2025-09-16
OpenJDK Runtime Environment GraalVM CE 25+37.1 (build 25+37-jvmci-b01)
OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM GraalVM CE 25+37.1 (build 25+37-jvmci-b01, mixed mode, sharing)
```

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.29.0

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

```
Apache Maven 3.9.11 (3e54c93a704957b63ee3494413a2b544fd3d825b)
Maven home: /home/user/.m2/wrapper/dists/apache-maven-3.9.11/a2d47e15
Java version: 25, vendor: GraalVM Community, runtime: /home/user/.sdkman/candidates/java/25-graalce
Default locale: en_US, platform encoding: UTF-8
OS name: "linux", version: "6.17.6-arch1-1", arch: "amd64", family: "unix"
```

### Additional information

The relevant server logs including a full stack trace are included below:

quarkusio/quarkus#50901.body:
### Describe the bug

While migrating one of our services to Quarkus we noticed that a custom SSLContext was ignored when quarkus.tls.trust-all is enabled.

The root cause was [this line](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/046530e9ba43d6e429ddb466d8737e18874eed69/extensions/resteasy-classic/resteasy-client/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/restclient/runtime/QuarkusRestClientBuilder.java#L388) in the Quarkus code that simply uses the standard SSLContext

### Expected behavior

Custom SSLContext is maintained

### Actual behavior

Custom SSLContext is ignored

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.15.7

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

Given this code has not changed for 3+ years I assume it is still the same with latest Quarkus version
quarkusio/quarkus#50901.title: When quarkus.tls.trust-all is enabled the custom SSLContext is ignored

quarkusio/quarkus#50913.body:
### Describe the bug

When i insert a Kafka message with duplicate headers, which happens if you use SmallRye's KafkaRetryTopic failure strategy and seems to be intended (see comment @ https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-reactive-messaging/issues/3209#issuecomment-3479181324) into an Kafka topic, then Quarkus Dev UI isnt able to show the whole topic.

### Expected behavior

Quarkus Dev UI (http://localhost:8080/q/dev-ui/quarkus-kafka-client/topics) should be able to show topics regardless of message content.

### Actual behavior

Quarkus Dev UI crashes with following stacktrace:

`

	at io.quarkus.kafka.client.runtime.dev.ui.model.converter.KafkaModelConverter.convert(KafkaModelConverter.java:28)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:197)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList$ArrayListSpliterator.forEachRemaining(ArrayList.java:1708)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:509)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:499)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReduceOps$ReduceOp.evaluateSequential(ReduceOps.java:921)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:234)

	at io.quarkus.devui.runtime.comms.JsonRpcRouter.lambda$invoke$12(JsonRpcRouter.java:346)
	at io.smallrye.mutiny.unchecked.UncheckedSupplier.lambda$toSupplier$0(UncheckedSupplier.java:41)
	at io.smallrye.context.impl.wrappers.SlowContextualSupplier.get(SlowContextualSupplier.java:21)
	at io.smallrye.mutiny.operators.uni.builders.UniCreateFromItemSupplier.subscribe(UniCreateFromItemSupplier.java:28)

`

### How to Reproduce?

1) Insert Kafka message with duplicate headers into your topic (provided by Dev UI)
2) Visit http://localhost:8080/q/dev-ui/quarkus-kafka-client/topics
3) Click on topic
4) Site does not load and console log shows exception.

quarkusio/quarkus#50936.body:
### Describe the bug

When using the lombok gradle plugin with the `quarkusTest` task , the live reload fails with

```
Compilation Failed:
/src/main/java/org/acme/GreetingResource.java:8: error: package lombok.extern.slf4j does not exist
import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
```

This does not happen when running `quarkusDev` , and in continuous testing with `quarkusDev`

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer : [https://github.com/Malandril/quarkus-bug-lombok-test](https://github.com/Malandril/quarkus-bug-lombok-test)

run `./gradlew quarkusTest`

Then change anything in the `GreetingResource` class, it wail fail with a compilation error


### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

21

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.29.1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

9.1.0

### Additional information

It is caused by #50253 , more precisely the commit 1ed554b6abc831138610d808703d9bf8f0b1f8b0 , reverting it fixes the issue.
quarkusio/quarkus#50936.title: Gradle QuarkusTest fails with Compilation Failed when using lombok on reload

quarkusio/quarkus#50939.body:
### Description

Sometimes users combine OIDC to login users and smallrye-jwt to verify tokens that were created in the application code after a user login, returned to the client, and resubmitted as bearer access tokens with a custom header to distinguish such tokens.

OIDC has an option to verify tokens with a locally configured public key. 

A tenant resolver that can check a tenant-header property can link to such OIDC configuration

### Implementation ideas

I believe we can check a single header, I'm not sure it is practical to select tenants by checking multiple headers, users can create custom TenantConfigResolver if needed
quarkusio/quarkus#50939.title: Add static OIDC TenantResolver that checks headers

quarkusio/quarkus#50943.body:
### Description

Starting with rabbitmq 4, amqp 1.0 is natively supported.
https://www.rabbitmq.com/blog/2024/08/05/native-amqp

This lifts the limitations listed in:
https://quarkus.io/guides/rabbitmq-reference 

### Implementation ideas


Maybe the guide could be updated to add a 4.0 section. Not sure if documentation for rabbitmq 3.13 should be outright deleted.

The devservice image should be updated to the 4.0 series.
quarkusio/quarkus#50943.title: Update reactive messaging rabbitmq for native amqp 1 support

quarkusio/quarkus#50947.body:

Works in `3.25.4` fails in `3.29.2`. the code: ```java import io.smallrye.mutiny.Uni; import io.vertx.core.json.JsonObject; import io.vertx.ext.web.RoutingContext; import io.quarkus.vertx.web.Body; import io.quarkus.vertx.web.Route; import io.quarkus.vertx.web.Route.HttpMethod; import jakarta.annotation.security.PermitAll; import jakarta.enterprise.context.ApplicationScoped; import jakarta.ws.rs.core.MediaType; import jakarta.validation.Valid; @ApplicationScoped public class ReactiveRoutesReproducer { @PermitAll @Route(path = "/hello", methods = HttpMethod.GET) Uni hello() { return Uni.createFrom().item("Hello World"); } /** * Echo URL that allows for testing the request body and headers * * @param body JsonObject * @param ctx RoutingContext * @return JsonObject */ @PermitAll @Route(path = "/echo", methods = HttpMethod.POST, consumes = MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON, produces = MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON) Uni echo(@Valid @Body JsonObject body, RoutingContext ctx) { ctx.user() != null ? ctx.user().principal() : new JsonObject().put("name", "anonymous"); result.put("user", principal); result.put("path", ctx.request().uri()); result.put("headers", headers); quarkusio/quarkus#50955.body:
### Describe the bug

I have an app (https://github.com/edeandrea/non-deterministic-no-problem) that builds/tests fine with Quarkus 3.28.4, but when upgrading to 3.29.x it starts breaking with

`Entity 'org.parasol.model.audit.ServiceCompleteAuditEvent' was not found. Did you forget to annotate your Panache Entity classes with '@Entity'?`

The entities have `@Entity` and use inheritance with discriminator values

https://github.com/edeandrea/non-deterministic-no-problem/blob/main/src/main/java/org/parasol/model/audit/ServiceStartedAuditEvent.java for example is one of the entities

I didn't see anything related in the release notes



### Expected behavior

I would expect upgrading to Quarkus 3.29.x to work

### Actual behavior

It doesn't

### How to Reproduce?

1. Clone https://github.com/edeandrea/non-deterministic-no-problem)
2. `./mvnw test -Dtest=AuditEventRepositoryTests`
3. Run `./mvnw test -Dtest=AuditEventRepositoryTests -Dquarkus.platform.version=3.28.4` to see it succeed

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

 - [x] Each time Vert.x instance is retrieved from CDI there is a message logged on INFO : 
INFO  [io.apicurio.registry.client.RegistryClientRequestAdapterFactory] (executor-thread-1) Successfully retrieved a Vertx instance from CDI.

- [x] There are a bunch of class names in the Quarkus processor for native compilation configuration that possibly no longer exist, we need to clean those up
- [x] Verify different auth methods including OAuth working in JVM and Native mode
- [x] Migration guide for config changes etc. + yaml files for quarkus update command

quarkusio/quarkus#51017.body:
### Description

Sometimes you want to show the version or another property from the Maven POM inside the documentation of Quarkus. Right now you have to make sure you also update the relevant values in the documentation.

This for example happens when we reference the version of a devservice container in the documentation. See this PR for example were we update Keycloak: https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51014

You have to set the version in the pom:
`<keycloak.server.version>26.4.5</keycloak.server.version>`

And also update the relevant documentation. There was even a case which had been forgotten in the past, or got mixed up with the Keycloak admin client version.

### Implementation ideas

Currently I have no good understanding of how the documentation is built and if this is already possible somehow. If that's the case please let me know.

I can imagine we could use Qute to do this for us, or during the `process-resources` lifecycle of Maven.
quarkusio/quarkus#51017.title: Support linking POM properties from Maven inside documentation

quarkusio/quarkus#51042.body:
Hello,

In your company, we encountered a problem with the Mailer (`quarkus-mailer` / blocking). Under certain conditions (probably misconfigured firewall/postfix), it appears to wait indefinitely, thereby blocking the thread. This blockage causes other side effects, such as the appearance of WARN messages concerning the SQL transaction in progress, which has been open for several minutes, and Kafka rebalancing after 5 minutes.

It could be interesting to have a way to configure timeout when using blocking mailer to avoid this problem. Maybe the `BlockingMailerImpl` must avoid usage of `await().indefinitely()`.

https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/d7922be743494173c1dddf5f23a6d0d0d54fe0eb/extensions/mailer/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/mailer/runtime/BlockingMailerImpl.java#L20

quarkusio/quarkus#51083.body:
`quarkus.oidc.token-state-manager` configuration group and `quarkus.oidc.token-state-manager.encryption-required` in particular apply to the default token state manager which stores tokens in a cookie. Custom token state managers that store tokens on the server side, incl OIDC DB and Redis ones shipped with Quarkus do not encrypt tokens. However, as noted in #51082, it was expected that OIDC DB manager would also have tokens encrypted, even if stored on the server side. I think we should consider applying default OIDC TokenStateManager token encryption configuration to manage token encryption for all token state managers. The only concern in some cases it can be considered a breaking change. This can happen when an encryption key is generated and does not survive a restart, so after a restart, tokens stored in DB or Redis state manager wont be decrypted. It can also be considered an unexpected change where DB is secured, with the token encryption possibly significantly increasing the required storage capacity. Overall though it should harden token security, minimize risks where a DB or some cache state is accessed out of band, and make it consistent, so all in all, making it a breaking change by encrypting it by default for non-default providers makes sense IMHO, and users would only need to do `quarkus.oidc.token-state-manager.encryption-required=false` to keep their custom DB/Redis/etc managers keeping tokens in the clear form. ### Discussed in https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/discussions/51082
Originally posted by **mschorsch** November 18, 2025 Hello, we use [Database TokenStateManager](https://quarkus.io/guides/security-oidc-code-flow-authentication#db-token-state-manager) and expected that when the property `quarkus.oidc.token-state-manager.encryption-required` is enabled, the tokens would be stored in encrypted form in the database. Is this not the case? Is this a bug? cc @sberyozkin
quarkusio/quarkus#51083.title: Apply default OIDC TokenStateManager token encryption configuration to custom TokenStateManagers

quarkusio/quarkus#51095.body:
### Describe the bug

https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51009 hardcodes docker api version to 1.44 (change done with https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/51002).


`ResettableSystemProperties` were used to instantiate TestContainers and 
[DefaultDockerClientConfig](https://github.com/docker-java/docker-java/blob/main/docker-java-core/src/main/java/com/github/dockerjava/core/DefaultDockerClientConfig.java#L236) takes system properties with highest priority.

This is unfortunately a breaking and non-fixable change for Docker < 25 due to api incompatibility as there is no way for me to influence the `api.version` property passed to `docker-java`.

I can not currently use newer Docker engine version as I am running builds on BuildJet.

Would it be possible to at least respect `api.version` system property if it is set? Or provide an alternative property / env var.

I have also mentioned this issue in testcontainers repo (https://github.com/testcontainers/testcontainers-java/issues/11232), as they have also made a breaking change with 2.0.2 (but in that case I was able to overwrite `api.version` property). Seems the best course of action would be if they implemented version negotiation.

_No response_

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#51101.body:
### Describe the bug

While hiding deprecated configuration properties from extension documentation is arguably a good thing when there is a better replacement (e.g. in case of renaming), it can be confusing, for example when:

* someone finds a property in an old app, and wonders what that property does
* the property is intended for migration purposes only, and thus is deprecated right from the start, or soon after being introduced (see https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51006#issuecomment-3540484293)

Maybe we could find a different way to handle deprecated properties, e.g. marking them with a "deprecated" tag, or styling them as "strikethrough" in the docs?

### Expected behavior

Deprecated configuration properties are documented, though advertised as deprecated.

### Actual behavior

Deprecated configuration properties are not documented at all.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Look for `quarkus.transaction-manager.unsafe-multiple-last-resources` in the configuration reference at https://quarkus.io/guides/datasource
2. Don't find it
3. Be confused, because it exists: 
https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/2fbc83dc745f870a75560eec67e2bc01a4eb34c6/extensions/narayana-jta/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/narayana/jta/runtime/TransactionManagerBuildTimeConfig.java#L35-L37

quarkusio/quarkus#51106.body:
`quarkus-bom` in versions from at least 3.27.0 to the lastest 3.29.3 manages non-existent versions of `org.jboss:jandex` assuming that they were relocated to `io.smallrye:jandex` but in fact they were not.

I see several options how we could solve the problem:

(a) Stop managing `org.jboss:jandex`
(b) Manage `org.jboss:jandex` at the last working version 3.3.1
(c) Publish the missing relocations for the versions after 3.3.1 and ensure they will be published in the future

I'd vote for (a) because we are actively banning `org.jboss:jandex` through Maven enforcer, so the transitives of Quarkus and all Quarkiverse projects enforcing [Quarkus bans](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/independent-projects/enforcer-rules/src/main/resources/enforcer-rules/quarkus-banned-dependencies.xml#L104) are free of `org.jboss:jandex`.

Generally, This is not the first time that `quarkus-bom` manages a non-existent dependency. There should be a test in place ensuring that all BOM entries are resolvable.
quarkusio/quarkus#51106.title: `quarkus-bom` manages a non-existent version of `org.jboss:jandex` after jandex stopped relocating after 3.3.1

quarkusio/quarkus#51111.body:
### Description

As part of the plan described in https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/discussions/51041, it's clear that it would be very useful if Maven extensions would be generally used.

We'll have fallbacks in place explaining what needs to be done alternatively, but if the extensions are enabled:

- we can automatically apply the settings we need
- it's future proof: should settings need further tuning, users won't need to adapt their hardcoded settings but rely on our maven extension to have been updated

The good news: our code generation template already enables them. What I'm proposing is to add a further check, which provides clear instructions about what to do to enable them, if they are not already.

### Implementation ideas

It's a fairly trivial improvement on `QuarkusBootstrapMojo`.
quarkusio/quarkus#51111.title: More strongly encourage users to enable Maven extensions

quarkusio/quarkus#51133.body:
### Describe the bug

When setting audience to be included in jwt using `quarkus.oidc-client.credentials.jwt.audience`, 
and the audience ends with a slash `/` 
then quarkus strips that slash from aud in the jwt before sending the token request - causing server side aud validation to fail.

### Expected behavior

When setting audience to be included in jwt using quarkus.oidc-client.credentials.jwt.audience, the aud in the jwt should be exactly like the specified one in properties.

### Actual behavior

I set this:
`quarkus.oidc-client.credentials.jwt.audience: "https://maskinporten.dev/"`

But the validation at maskinporten fails, reporting invalid aud in jwt. When inspecting jwt, I can see that the one sent from my Quarkus app actually contains:
`"aud": "https://maskinporten.dev"`



### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

It would seem we (currently) manage netty to version `4.1.128.Final`, and almost all Netty libraries  we have are being managed to correctly match this, however in the output of a `mvn dependency:tree` I also see some components by Netty `4.1.125.Final` .

This seems to trigger because `independent-projects/vertx-utils/pom.xml` is in the "independent-projects" section - could we improve on this structure to avoid similar mistakes in the future?

quarkusio/quarkus#51196.body:
### Describe the bug

The check for `abstract` methods declared in decorators, as present in `Decorators.createDecorator()`, is imprecise. We check that decorators do not declare _any_ `abstract` methods, but the spec says that decorators may not declare `abstract` methods that do not belong to a decorated type. Also, we only check methods declared on the decorator class itself, not inherited methods. (We can probably ignore `abstract` methods inherited from interfaces, because those are decorated types, but a decorator can inherit `abstract` methods from a superclass, and those do not necessarily have to be present on decorated types.)

That doesn't cause any TCK failures, because decorators are not present in CDI Lite (plus I'm not sure this is actually tested by the TCK), and so far there hasn't been any issues filed about this, so this is purely of a theoretical concern (I only stumbled upon this by writing test cases for various corner cases). Still, it is a deviation from the specification, so I believe we should fix it. It's a lower priority kind of thing, though.

### Expected behavior

Only fail when an `abstract` method that doesn't belong to a decorated type is explicitly declared on a decorator or inherited (and not implemented!) from a supertype.

### Actual behavior

Fail whenever an `abstract` method is explicitly declared on a decorator; `abstract` methods declared on supertypes are ignored.

### How to Reproduce?

N/A

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

_No response_

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#51206.body:
### Description

In Hibernate Extensions tests (both blocking and reactive) the test configuration is currently split between two mechanisms:

* `application.properties` files
* `overrideConfigKey` calls inside the test code

`overrideConfigKey` is preferable as it keeps configuration close to the test and makes test intent explicit

`application.properties` files are still used due to test container connection string interpolation. It's the only way to inject the connection string from the Maven properties expressed like this

```
quarkus.datasource.reactive.url=${postgres.reactive.url}
```

The `${key}` syntax won't work inside Java code.

However, after the merge of PR [https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51066](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51066), tests use dev services, so we don't need a test container connection string URL anymore.

As a result, we can stop using `application.properties` in these tests and rely solely on `overrideConfigKey`, which should simplify the test



### Implementation ideas

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#51206.title: Use `overrideConfigKey` to test Hibernate tests instead of application.properties files

quarkusio/quarkus#51229.body:
### Description

Currently, some DB providers are set up to allow max text column sizes, some 4 or 5K chars. Also, refresh tokens can often occupy a significantly less amount of space compared to the one occupied by the id token.

Users should have an option to tune these column sizes, either to optimize storage or avoid overflows in some cases. 

### Implementation ideas

Add build-time configuration to set `id-token-column-size`, `access-token-column-size` and `refresh-token-column-size`.
quarkusio/quarkus#51229.title: Let OIDC DbTokenStateManager users control column size for storing tokens

quarkusio/quarkus#51251.body:
### Describe the bug

The default quarkus-app client does not set the web origins property, it should be present:

```
    [...]
    "secret" : "secret",
    "redirectUris" : [ "*" ],
    "webOrigins" : [ "*" ],
    [...]
```



### Expected behavior

I would expect Keycloak + Swagger integration to work out of the box

### Actual behavior

Keycloak + Swagger do not work out of the box, it reports a CORS errors in the web console.

In Swagger UI: `auth errorTypeError: NetworkError when attempting to fetch resource.`
In the console: `Cross-Origin Request Blocked: The Same Origin Policy disallows reading the remote resource at http://localhost:32842/realms/quarkus/protocol/openid-connect/token?nonce=58884406-367e-4050-a91c-d4ca3d6aff2a. (Reason: CORS header ‘Access-Control-Allow-Origin’ missing). Status code: 200.`

### How to Reproduce?

- `git clone https://github.com/BrianSetz/quarkus-keycloak-swagger-reproducer.git`
- Start quarkus-dev
- Open dev-ui
- Go to SwaggerUI
- Try to Authenticate (alice/alice)


### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.29.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

I would be happy to contribute this, but I am not sure where the original realm.json is located? I searched a bit and found these but they appear to be in the integration tests? https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Aquarkusio%2Fquarkus%20%22realm%22%20%3A%20%22quarkus%22%2C&type=code
quarkusio/quarkus#51251.title: Keycloak's default quarkus-app client is missing  `"webOrigins" : [ "+" ],`

quarkusio/quarkus#51266.body:
### Describe the bug

Executing quarkus-maven-plugin to generate code sometimes fail with error `Could not acquire lock(s)` during maven artifact resolution (stacktrace bellow). This happens only occasionally as it is probably a data race, but if fails our tests pretty often.

There is a workaround around this by setting `-Dquarkus.bootstrap.blocking-task-runner=true`. But setting this is not a proper solution to a problem that can randomly happen basically anywhere. Also this option/switch is AFAIK not documented anywhere.

quarkusio/quarkus#51269.body:
Re-occurrence of #46048. Originally reported (along with an unrelated issue) in https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/50740.

```
org.junit.jupiter.engine.execution.ConditionEvaluationException: Failed to evaluate condition [io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension]: Internal error: Test class was loaded with an unexpected classloader or the thread context classloader was incorrect.
  at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:197)

  at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyIntoWithCancel(AbstractPipeline.java:527)

  ... 14 more

  at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader.fail(ServiceLoader.java:593)
  at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader$LazyClassPathLookupIterator.hasNextService(ServiceLoader.java:1244)

```

I believe we're seeing this again because something in Surefire 3.5.4 exposes the problem, and we upgraded to that version in September (https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/50092). Unfortunately, that update was needed to fix #50076, so if we downgraded to 3.5.3, it would re-expose that problem. 

I've confirmed that https://github.com/apache/maven-surefire/pull/863 is what's causing us problems, so I'll have a look to see if I can make our test launching co-exist with that change. I don't see many options for making it work, unfortunately. Alternatively, we could ask Surefire to revert the problem PR.

quarkusio/quarkus#51281.body:
### Describe the bug

Running tests which replace a Kafka connector with an InMemory connector do not work after updating to 3.30. 

after running `InMemoryConnector.switchIncomingChannelsToInMemory("prices")`


```

java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: SRMSG18304: Unknown channel prices

	at io.smallrye.reactive.messaging.memory.InMemoryConnector.source(InMemoryConnector.java:195)
	at io.smallrye.reactive.messaging.memory.InMemoryConnector_Subclass.source$$superforward(Unknown Source)
	at io.smallrye.reactive.messaging.memory.InMemoryConnector_Subclass$1.apply(Unknown Source)
	at io.quarkus.arc.impl.AroundInvokeInvocationContext.proceed(AroundInvokeInvocationContext.java:73)
	at io.quarkus.arc.impl.AroundInvokeInvocationContext.proceed(AroundInvokeInvocationContext.java:62)
	at io.quarkus.smallrye.reactivemessaging.runtime.DuplicatedContextConnectorFactoryInterceptor.intercept(DuplicatedContextConnectorFactoryInterceptor.java:37)
	at io.quarkus.smallrye.reactivemessaging.runtime.DuplicatedContextConnectorFactoryInterceptor_Bean.intercept(Unknown Source)
	at io.quarkus.arc.impl.InterceptorInvocation.invoke(InterceptorInvocation.java:42)
	at io.quarkus.arc.impl.AroundInvokeInvocationContext.perform(AroundInvokeInvocationContext.java:30)
	at io.quarkus.arc.impl.InvocationContexts.performAroundInvoke(InvocationContexts.java:27)
	at io.smallrye.reactive.messaging.memory.InMemoryConnector_Subclass.source(Unknown Source)
	at io.smallrye.reactive.messaging.memory.InMemoryConnector_ClientProxy.source(Unknown Source)
	at MyTest.test(MyTest.java:29)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:580)
	at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.runExtensionMethod(QuarkusTestExtension.java:998)
	at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.interceptTestMethod(QuarkusTestExtension.java:846)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1596)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1596)
```

### Expected behavior

We can replace the channel with an InMemory channel and the tests run as before.




### Actual behavior

```
    public <T> InMemorySource<T> source(String channel) {
        if (channel == null) {
            throw InMemoryExceptions.ex.illegalArgumentChannelMustNotBeNull();
        }
        InMemorySourceImpl<?> source = sources.get(channel);
        if (source == null) {
            throw InMemoryExceptions.ex.illegalArgumentUnknownChannel(channel);
        }
```
here the `sources` map is empty where in contrast with the older version 3.29.x there are sources in there. So in case of the new version the exception it thrown.

### How to Reproduce?

Reporducer: https://github.com/spike83/quarkus-smallrye-in-memory-3.30-reporducer

I marked the version working and the version not working in the pom.xml.

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.0, 3.30.1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#51330.body:
### Describe the bug

Using CSRF programmatic setup API from https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/49618. When I setup an app with programmatic CSRF setup, as described [here](https://quarkus.io/guides/security-csrf-prevention#csrf-prevention-programmatic-set-up). After starting the app it will fail with:
```
...
Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: Please add an extension that provides a CSRF prevention feature, for example the Quarkus REST Cross-Site Request Forgery Prevention `quarkus-rest-csrf` extension
...
```
even though I have the extension `io.quarkus:quarkus-rest-csrf` added.

### Expected behavior

App will start without problem.

### Actual behavior

App crashes during start.

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer - https://github.com/mocenas/reproducer-quarkus-csrf-not-working
Try to run it with `quarkus dev`

Reproducer created from guide https://quarkus.io/guides/security-csrf-prevention just by steps:
```
quarkus create app org.acme:security-csrf-prevention \
    --extension='rest-csrf' \
    --no-code
cd security-csrf-prevention
```
Add `csrfToken.html` and `UserNameResource`.
Add class [CsrfProgrammaticConfig](https://quarkus.io/guides/security-csrf-prevention#csrf-prevention-programmatic-set-up).
Run with `quarkus dev`

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

17

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.1  (or also 999-SNAPSHOT)

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#51332.body:
### Describe the bug

It was always possible to set certain options for OIDC with environment variables. This is especially useful for setting some options for your production environment.

In our case we set `QUARKUS_OIDC_AUTHENTICATION_SESSION_AGE_EXTENSION=23h` to extend the lifetime of the cookie to make sure we can refresh tokens accordingly with Azure Entra ID (those tokens are valid for 1 hour by default). See https://quarkus.io/guides/security-oidc-configuration-properties-reference#quarkus-oidc_quarkus-oidc-authentication-session-age-extension

Setting this via normal application properties or yaml config does work. However it no longer works with the environment variable in Quarkus 3.29.4 after updating from 3.26.3

### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

_No response_

### Describe the bug

Setting CSRF token-size to any other size than 16 (default) causes the rendered form to return 400 - Bad request.
Doesn't matter if it is set by property `quarkus.rest-csrf.token-size=17` or via [programmatic API](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/49618)



### Expected behavior

_No response_

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

Repurposed https://github.com/mocenas/reproducer-quarkus-csrf-not-working from #51330 
Starting the app with `quarkus dev` and accessing http://localhost:8080/service/csrfTokenForm fails to render the page with 400 - bad request. 
Setting the token size to 16 in `CsrfProgrammaticConfig` or ommiting tokenSize option entirely makes it work again.

quarkusio/quarkus#51359.body:
By fixing #46092 we added support for dealing with GraalVM `--future-defaults` values by making necessary build time adjustments depending on the option selected.

More specifically, in #46092 we support `--future-defaults` values passed in via the `quarkus.native.additional-build-args` or `quarkus.native.additional-build-args-append` configuration options. These can be passed as properties, environment variables...etc.

However, GraalVM itself has an additional way in which native image arguments can be passed in. That is via the `NATIVE_IMAGE_OPTIONS` environment variable. This option is what the oracle/graal repository uses to verify if `--future-defaults` works well (see [here](https://github.com/oracle/graal/blob/master/.github/workflows/quarkus-future-defaults.yml)).

So when Quarkus checks the configuration to see if future default values are set, it should also verify the `NATIVE_IMAGE_OPTIONS` environment variable. The aim of this task is to do this exclusively for future defaults work, but if needed it could be shared by any other logic that depends on parsing native additional build arguments.
quarkusio/quarkus#51359.title: Future defaults option parsing needs to check NATIVE_IMAGE_OPTIONS env variable

quarkusio/quarkus#51378.body:
`QuarkusComponentTest` fails with ambiguous dependencies if bean is injected with parameterized qualifier multiple times

### Describe the bug

We have a project which connects to RabbitMQ using the SmallRye Reactive Messaging extension. One class injects two emitters on different channels.

When trying to test the class using `@QuarkusComponentTest`, the test fails to start due to ambiguous dependencies. Arc seems to produce both synthetic beans but cannot distinguish between them anymore (which is possible when running Quarkus itself).

I created a minimal reproducer using a custom qualifier.

### Expected behavior

The test class starts and runs the actual tests.

### Actual behavior

The test discovery fails with an error:

```
Caused by: jakarta.enterprise.inject.spi.DeploymentException: Found 2 deployment problems:

### Describe the bug

We encountered a strange problem with the Lambda extension when upgrading to 3.30.2.

When starting or running the application in the tests, we get the following error:

```
        Caused by:
        java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to start quarkus
            at io.quarkus.runner.ApplicationImpl.doStart(Unknown Source)
            at io.quarkus.runtime.Application.start(Application.java:101)
            at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:580)
            at io.quarkus.runner.bootstrap.StartupActionImpl.run(StartupActionImpl.java:350)
            at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.doJavaStart(QuarkusTestExtension.java:232)
            at io.quarkus.test.junit.QuarkusTestExtension.ensureStarted(QuarkusTestExtension.java:640)

                at io.quarkus.amazon.lambda.runtime.AmazonLambdaRecorder.chooseHandlerClass(AmazonLambdaRecorder.java:102)
                at io.quarkus.runner.recorded.AmazonLambdaProcessor$recordHandlerClass501835388.deploy_0(Unknown Source)
                at io.quarkus.runner.recorded.AmazonLambdaProcessor$recordHandlerClass501835388.deploy(Unknown Source)
                ... 8 more
```

There are now 2 `LambdaConfig` classes in the same package `io.quarkus.amazon.lambda.runtime`.

This change comes from this [commit](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/commit/b11eadc73606e5ae95db2c52957080e0c88e7706#diff-b9bdd85bf39a754f640325e3e952e8f951d8f53c903bfc19d5921992f621086d). 

I assume it's related to the problem?

quarkusio/quarkus#51421.body:
### Description

The Kubernetes extension is quite central to Quarkus. It has evolved with Quarkus itself quite organically. It is now quite complex and still contains compatibility layers for things that have been deprecated for more than 5 years. It would be quite beneficial to remove these compatibility layers to simplify the code and its maintenance. 

Another aspect is the fact that it relies on [dekorate](http://dekorate.io), a project that is now more or less in maintenance mode, creating a fragile base to keep building on.

Finally, [work on moving the Kubernetes client dev service the new architecture](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/49944) has uncovered fundamental incompatibilities with how the Kubernetes extension and the new Dev Service architecture function and their respective assumptions.

This issue is meant as a starting point to discuss work on the evolution of the Kubernetes support in Quarkus.

/cc @cescoffier @geoand @holly-cummins @ozangunalp 

### Analysis

Preliminary analysis of dekorate usage is that it would be a significant effort to remove it from the Kubernetes extension for several reasons:
- it's pretty deeply embedded in how the extension functions. 
- it's exposed externally to 3rd party extensions via build items and is, at the very least, used in the Helm extension
quarkusio/quarkus#51421.title: Kubernetes extension evolution

quarkusio/quarkus#51434.body:
### Description

Introduce an `ModuleOpenBuildItem` build item to let extensions control the need to have "--add-opens" instructions to be applied to the runtime.

This needs to influence consistently:
 - dev-mode
 - production modes (fast jar and uber-jar formats)
 - integration tests (Maven, Gradle, IDEA launchers)

And ideally while not needing any action from the user.


### Implementation ideas

See https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/discussions/51041
quarkusio/quarkus#51434.title: Introduce a ModuleOpenBuildItem to let extensions control --add-opens needs for the runtime

quarkusio/quarkus#51437.body:
### Description

The work on #49920 introduced a `ModuleOpenBuildItem`, which dynamically applies Java module configurations to the JVM launched for dev-mode or integration tests.
This needs either our agent to be attached, or some JVM flags to allow us to take control of such aspects; when working on #49920 we made sure that a Maven build would automatically set such flags; when they are not, things will still work but there will be actionable warnings logged, so any Gradle user won't have problems either but they might need to add some more JVM flags to their build scripts.

It would be ideal to adapt our Gradle plugins to setup such flags automatically, so that users don't need to bother with any action at all.

### Implementation ideas

See https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/49920/commits/a3387cb21629bcd90b77403de84acf7aa64ad5e8
quarkusio/quarkus#51437.title: Automatically set recommended JVM flags in Gradle builds

quarkusio/quarkus#51456.body:
### Describe the bug

When using OpenTelemetry with the MySQL JDBC driver, the following error appears in the logs:

```
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app] Caused by: java.lang.IllegalStateException: GlobalOpenTelemetry.set has already been called. GlobalOpenTelemetry.set must be called only once before any calls to GlobalOpenTelemetry.get. If you are using the OpenTelemetrySdk, use OpenTelemetrySdkBuilder.buildAndRegisterGlobal instead. Previous invocation set to cause of this exception.
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app]    at io.opentelemetry.api.GlobalOpenTelemetry.set(GlobalOpenTelemetry.java:108)
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app]    at io.opentelemetry.api.GlobalOpenTelemetry.set(GlobalOpenTelemetry.java:131)
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app]    at io.opentelemetry.sdk.autoconfigure.AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdkBuilder.build(AutoConfiguredOpenTelemetrySdkBuilder.java:433)
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app]    at io.quarkus.opentelemetry.runtime.OpenTelemetryRecorder$2.apply(OpenTelemetryRecorder.java:117)
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app]    at io.quarkus.opentelemetry.runtime.OpenTelemetryRecorder$2.apply(OpenTelemetryRecorder.java:90)
04:44:08,320 INFO  [app]    at io.opentelemetry.api.OpenTelemetry_8sUBW77j05ZkJQ-41BlGlCO8EKA_Synthetic_Bean.createSynthetic(Unknown Source)
```


After a deep investigation, I found that MySQLJ has a class [`com.mysql.cj.otel.OpenTelemetryHandler`](https://github.com/mysql/mysql-connector-j/blob/release/9.x/src/main/core-impl/java/com/mysql/cj/otel/OpenTelemetryHandler.java#L67) which initializes the `GlobalOpenTelemetry` prematurely and breaks the integration

No error

quarkusio/quarkus#51501.body:
### Describe the bug

Before Quarkus 3.30.3 whenever a quarkus messaging channel was disabled (`enabled=false`) the enforcement of required configs were also disabled. This is no longer the case.

Example

```
mp:
  messaging:
    incoming:
      some-channel:
        enabled: false
        connector: smallrye-kafka
        topic: ""
```

Before 3.30.3 the above config was allowed. Now deployment will fail with:

```
java.util.NoSuchElementException: SRCFG00040: The config property quarkus.messaging.incoming.some-channel.topic is defined as the empty String ("") which the following Converter considered to be null: io.smallrye.config.Converters$BuiltInConverter
```

If the channel is enabled, then failure makes sense.

### Expected behavior

Should deploy just fine (previous behavior)

### Actual behavior

Fails with

```
java.util.NoSuchElementException: SRCFG00040: The config property quarkus.messaging.incoming.some-channel.topic is defined as the empty String ("") which the following Converter considered to be null: io.smallrye.config.Converters$BuiltInConverter
```


### How to Reproduce?

Create an channel that is disabled and set one of its required fields to empty string.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Darwin XYZ 25.0.0 Darwin Kernel Version 25.0.0: Wed Sep 17 21:41:26 PDT 2025; root:xnu-12377.1.9~141/RELEASE_ARM64_T6041 arm64

quarkusio/quarkus#51541.body:
### Describe the bug

I run a Quarkus application on `https://settings.example.com` and an associated static web site on `https://www.example.com`.

Here's the CORS config:
``` properties
quarkus.http.cors.enabled=true
quarkus.http.cors.origins=https://www.example.com
quarkus.http.cors.methods=GET
quarkus.http.cors.access-control-allow-credentials=true
```

Notice that I do not allow `POST`, `PUT` or `DELETE` requests via CORS.

The goal here is that the web site can `GET` certain data from the application despite that application having a different origin (`settings` subdomain vs `www` subdomain).

CORS works fine. The problem occurs when the user is on the `settings` subdomain and clicks a button to change something. This results in a same-origin `POST` request (see also [this comment](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/29542#issuecomment-1330518692) in #29542).

### Expected behavior

After noticing that it's dealing with a same-origin request, `io.quarkus.vertx.http.runtime.cors.CORSFilter` should skip checking the method.

### Actual behavior

1. `io.quarkus.vertx.http.runtime.cors.CORSFilter` detects that it's a same-origin request: `isSameOrigin(request, origin)` returns `true`.
2. The filter accepts the origin of the request, setting `allowsOrigin` to true.
3. The filter rejects this _**same-origin non-CORS**_ request because `POST` is not allowed _**for CORS requests**_.

### How to Reproduce?

I will try to write a reproducer as soon as I find the time.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.19045.6466]

### Output of `java -version`

OpenJDK Runtime Environment Corretto-21.0.8.9.1 (build 21.0.8+9-LTS)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.2

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Maven 3.9.11

### Additional information

Though the method check is what breaks my configuration, it's not only that: I suspect that all the logic about allowed headers and credentials also should not apply to _**same-origin non-CORS requests**_.

However, one should probably research that. The specs may or may not say something like "it's illegal / frowned upon / bandwidth wasting / harmless to return CORS-related response headers for non-CORS requests".
quarkusio/quarkus#51541.title: CORS filter complains about "wrong" method on same-origin POST request

quarkusio/quarkus#51542.body:
### Describe the bug

There's a bug where the feature which is generated for native image will miss some run time initialized classes when run time reinitialized classes are also configured.

Basically, the feature generated code creates an array that's large enough to hold every `Class` object in both the run-time-init and run-time-reinit lists. Then, there are two loops to populate the array. However, the second loop starts over at index 0, so the first `n` run-time-initialized classes get overwritten by run-time-reinitilized classes, causing what is likely to be a subtle misbehavior at run time when some classes are not run time initialized as expected. The remaining array elements just contain `null` in this case.

I'm doing a little Gizmo 1-to-Gizmo 2 rewriting and ran across this bug while analyzing the old code, so a fix will be coming soon.

quarkusio/quarkus#51574.body:
### Describe the bug

The QuarkusComponentTest's do not seem to use the configuration profiles (e.g. test) correctly.

For example, in the attached reproducer, I have configured in src/test/resources/application.properties:
```
%test.app.greeting=hello from application.properties
```

And my ConfigMapping:
```
@ConfigMapping(prefix = "app")
public interface GreetingMapping {
    @WithDefault("hello from mapping default")
    String greeting();
}
```

The test then uses the value from the default. I would have expected that the value from application.properties gets used, since a test is run.


If I change the application.properties as follows:
```
app.greeting=hello from application.properties
```
(i.e. remove the test profile), only then the value from the config gets used

### Expected behavior

config profile test is used by quarkuscomponenenttest


### Actual behavior

config profile test is used

Reproducer: https://github.com/Postremus/componenenttest-config-profile

Change src/test/resources/application.properties, remove the %test.

quarkusio/quarkus#51607.body:
### Describe the bug

I've annotated my REST resource classes with @AuthorizationPolicy.

Example:
```
@Path(/my-resource)
@AuthorizationPolicy(name = DefaultHttpSecurityPolicy.NAME)
public class MyResource {
    ...
}
```

I've also enabled Basic Authentication (but I have the same problem with OIDC token authentication) : 
`quarkus.http.auth.basic=true`

The problem is that once authenticated, the Swagger UI does not send the 'Authorization' header on requests.

It seems indeed the OpenAPI `security `attribute is only added on methods if I annotate my resource classes with `@Authenticated.`

But Quarkus does not allow to use both the `@Authenticated` and the `@AuthorizationPolicy` annotations : 

```
Class 'MyResource' is annotated with 'io.quarkus.vertx.http.security.AuthorizationPolicy' and 'io.quarkus.security.Authenticated' security annotations,
however security annotations cannot be combined.
```

So it is just impossible to have a working SwaggerUI when using @AuthorizationPolicy annotation

### Expected behavior

Using @AuthorizationPolicy on a resource class, should automatically mark its methods as secured in OpenAPI schema so that authentication through SwaggerUI can work.

### Actual behavior

Using @AuthorizationPolicy on a resource class, does not automatically mark its methods as secured in OpenAPI schema

### How to Reproduce?

quarkusio/quarkus#51637.body:
### Describe the bug

Creating loggers with dynamic logger names keeps them in memory and they are never gc-ed, possibly causing an OOM.

### Expected behavior

I expected that logger names that are not (anymore?) actively used are being garbage-collected.

### Actual behavior

Using the reproducer, I can see that the used memory is gradually increased, until an OOME is caused.

### How to Reproduce?

I have created a minimal project to showcase the issue here: https://github.com/JChrist/quarkusLoggerNameMemory

Mainly, it is a minimal project from https://code.quarkus.io, which, in the `ExampleResource.hello` endpoint, contains logic to create loggers in a loop and trigger a `System.gc()` every so often to monitor the memory situation/pressure.

In order to trigger the memory error, I compile the application with:
`./mvnw package`

and then execute it setting the max heap size to a very small value, e.g.:
`java -Xmx32m -jar target/quarkus-app/quarkus-run.jar`

quarkusio/quarkus#51642.body:
### Describe the bug

Quarkus 3.21.3 introduced a regression where the `quarkus-maven-plugin:generate-code-tests` errors for no valid reason.
This fails with the most recent release and was bisected to the above version using a command like the following on a quarkus project.

`mvn -ntp --batch-mode -DskipTests -f D:\source\github\myorg\project\bin\..\myquarkusapp clean package -Dquarkus-plugin.version=3.21.2`

whilst the path contains `..` this is not incorrect, merely unusual and can be constructed by various scripts that use the location of the absolute location of a script to obtain an absolute (but not canonical) location of something (like a quarkus project in this case).

Canonicalizing `D:\source\github\myorg\project\bin\..\myquarkusapp` to `D:\source\github\myorg\project\myquarkusapp` and using that as the file source causes the build to pass.

### Expected behavior

The build should pass even when given a non canonical location of the file

### Actual behavior

the build fails.

```
> mvn -ntp --batch-mode clean package -DskipTests -f %CD%\..\simple-project -Dquarkus.version=3.21.3

[INFO]

[INFO]
[INFO] --- quarkus:3.21.3:generate-code (default) @ quarkus-51642 ---
[INFO]
[INFO] --- compiler:3.13.0:compile (default-compile) @ quarkus-51642 ---
[INFO] No sources to compile
[INFO]
[INFO] --- quarkus:3.21.3:generate-code-tests (default) @ quarkus-51642 ---
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INFO] BUILD FAILURE
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[INFO] Total time:  2.250 s
[INFO] Finished at: 2025-12-18T20:15:34Z
[INFO] ------------------------------------------------------------------------
[ERROR] Failed to execute goal io.quarkus:quarkus-maven-plugin:3.21.3:generate-code-tests (default) on project quarkus-51642: Quarkus code generation phase has failed: Failed to initialize Quarkus Maven context: Failed to load current project at D:\source\github\quarkus-51642\simple-project\pom.xml: Failed to load project D:\source\github\quarkus-51642\simple-project\pom.xml -> [Help 1]
[ERROR]

```

### How to Reproduce?

* `mkdir simple-project`
* `cd simple-project`
* copy the following contents into `pom.xml`
```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">

  <artifactId>quarkus-51642</artifactId>
  <version>0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
  <description>https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/51642</description>

  <properties>
    <quarkus.version>3.30.4</quarkus.version>
    <quarkus-plugin.version>${quarkus.version}</quarkus-plugin.version>
    <quarkus.platform.version>${quarkus.version}</quarkus.platform.version>

        <type>pom</type>
        <scope>import</scope>
      </dependency>
    </dependencies>
  </dependencyManagement>

  <dependencies>
  </dependencies>

  <build>
    <plugins>
      <plugin>
        <groupId>io.quarkus</groupId>
        <artifactId>quarkus-maven-plugin</artifactId>
        <version>${quarkus-plugin.version}</version>
        <executions>
          <execution>
            <goals>
              <goal>generate-code</goal>
              <goal>generate-code-tests</goal>
              <goal>build</goal>
            </goals>
          </execution>
        </executions>
      </plugin>
    </plugins>
  </build>
</project>

```
 
* validate the project is valid `mvn clean package -DskipTests -f %CD% -Dquarkus.platform.version=3.21.3`
* use an abosolute but non canonical path to attempt to build the project `mvn clean package -DskipTests -f %CD%\..\simple-project Dquarkus.platform.version=3.21.3`

quarkusio/quarkus#51667.body:
### Describe the bug

The last clean build (when all quarkus native integration tests passed) was from December 18, 2025 here:
https://github.com/graalvm/mandrel/actions/runs/20321891451

This is using revision `e98bc519e1abd959bd1ffc8cf84b94100eadda0d` from quarkus.

Since December 19, 2025 or quarkus revision `614cd6f798646765ab0e1791528c820df4798cf7` all quarkus native integration tests fail:
https://github.com/graalvm/mandrel/actions/runs/20355898577

It doesn't seem GraalVM master revision related as I ran the same local GraalVM master build when verifying #51538. Now with a newer quarkus version it fails.

**Steps to reproduce:**

1. Grab a graal-master build from the passing CI run [here](https://github.com/graalvm/mandrel/actions/runs/20321891451/artifacts/4906257520)
2. Build quarkus main, revision `614cd6f798646765ab0e1791528c820df4798cf7` or newer.
3. Run any native integration test (e.g. `smallrye-config`)
4. Observe that the native binary doesn't start.

### Expected behavior

Quarkus native integration tests pass.

### Actual behavior

Quarkus native integration tests fail.
quarkusio/quarkus#51667.title: [Mandrel 25.0.x future-defaults=all, Graal master] All quarkus native integration tests fail since 2025-12-19

quarkusio/quarkus#51668.body:
### Describe the bug

To get CDI Interceptors working with beans created with `@Produces` (based on this comment https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/44815#issuecomment-2507302091) we wrapped those beans with an `InterceptionProxy`, which works fine in quarkus:dev mode.

When fully building an uber jar though, we get an error like this for any proxied bean that has multiple versions.

E.g. a sample class:
```
@ApplicationScoped
public class Config {
    @Produces
    @ApplicationScoped
    @Named("version1")
    public ExampleClass version1(InterceptionProxy<ExampleClass> proxy) {
        return proxy.create(new ExampleClass());
    }
    
    @Produces
    @ApplicationScoped
    @Named("version2")
    public ExampleClass version2(InterceptionProxy<ExampleClass> proxy) {
        return proxy.create(new ExampleClass());
    }
}
```


```
[ERROR] 	[error]: Build step io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.steps.JarResultBuildStep#buildRunnerJar threw an exception: IllegalStateException: Multiple GeneratedClassBuildItem were produced for the same classes:
[ERROR]
[ERROR] - com.example.package.ExampleClass_InterceptionSubclass: 2
[ERROR]
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.AbstractJarBuilder.checkConsistency(AbstractJarBuilder.java:92)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.AbstractJarBuilder.<init>(AbstractJarBuilder.java:76)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.UberJarBuilder.<init>(UberJarBuilder.java:77)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.steps.JarResultBuildStep.buildRunnerJar(JarResultBuildStep.java:120)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/java.lang.invoke.MethodHandle.invokeWithArguments(MethodHandle.java:733)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.deployment.ExtensionLoader$3.execute(ExtensionLoader.java:872)
[ERROR] 	at io.quarkus.builder.BuildContext.run(BuildContext.java:255)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.ContextHandler$1.runWith(ContextHandler.java:18)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2651)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2630)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor.runThreadBody(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1622)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1589)
[ERROR] 	at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
[ERROR] 	at org.jboss.threads.JBossThread.run(JBossThread.java:501)
```

Seems like this is probably a tricky problem to solve (proxies need to include their beans name in the class or something?), but it is the main use case I have personally for using the `@Produces` pattern to create multiple flavors of the same bean with different config or when using third party classes.

For now I'm just adding wrapper classes with different types to work around it.

### Expected behavior

Be able to create multiple versions of the same class with `InterceptionProxy`

### Actual behavior

buildRunnerJar throws an error during clean install.

### How to Reproduce?

Use InterceptorProxy to create 2 beans with the same class. Works in quarkus dev mode, fails when running an uber jar.

I can create a sample project if desired, didn't want to waste time if this is expected behavior with no plan to fix.

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

_No response_

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#51711.body:
I was experimenting with this project:
https://github.com/MikeEdgar/clik

And I noticed a lot of these warnings in the test output when running `./mvnw test -Dtest=TopicCommandTest` (`target/surefire-reports/io.streamshub.clik.command.topic.TopicCommandTest-output.txt`):

(don't do that at home yet as there's a massive memory leak going on...)

```
2025-12-23 15:36:10,123 WARN  [io.quarkus.runtime.configuration.ConfigRecorder] (main) Build time property cannot be changed at runtime:
 - quarkus.jacoco.title is set to 'null' but it is build time fixed to 'clik'. Did you change the property quarkus.jacoco.title after building the application?
```

It's something we should solve, I'm not sure if it's a problem of the JaCoCo extension or a more general issue in Quarkus.

/cc @radcortez
quarkusio/quarkus#51711.title: JaCoCo causes config warnings when running tests

quarkusio/quarkus#51724.body:
### Description

In extensions/reactive-routes/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/web/Route.java, the HttpMethod enum/class is missing support for the PATCH method, which is also a commonly used HTTP method for REST APIs.

Expected behavior:

PATCH should be included as a valid HttpMethod option.
PATCH routes should be supported similarly to other methods like GET, POST, PUT, etc.
Steps to reproduce:

Try to create a route using the PATCH method.
Note that it's not available or not functioning as expected.
Suggested fix: Add PATCH to the HttpMethod enum/class in Route.java and ensure it is handled correctly throughout the relevant codebase.

Additional context: PATCH is widely used for partial updates in REST APIs and should be supported for full HTTP compliance.

### Implementation ideas

    public static enum HttpMethod {
        GET,
        HEAD,
        POST,
        PUT,
        PATCH,
        DELETE,
        OPTIONS;
    }
quarkusio/quarkus#51724.title: Add PATCH to HttpMethod in Route.java

quarkusio/quarkus#51731.title: Infinispan timeout in native test

quarkusio/quarkus#51745.body:
### Describe the bug

This came up in the [Quarkus Superheroes + Quarkus main nightly build](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/23612#issuecomment-3691932897).

It seems that when using `quarkus.http.test-port=0` within an `@QuarkusIntegrationTest` isn't working anymore?

### Expected behavior

I would expect the integration test to start quarkus on a random port, but instead it doesn't. 

### Actual behavior

It tries to bind the app to port `0`.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Build Quarkus main
2. Clone https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus-super-heroes
3. `cd event-statistics`
4. `./mvnw clean verify -Dquarkus.platform.version=999-SNAPSHOT -Dquarkus.platform.group-id=io.quarkus -Dquarkus.platform.artifact-id=quarkus-bom`

@Entity
public class Forecast {

}

quarkusio/quarkus#51783.body:
### Describe the bug

I was trying to create a custom `HttpAuthenticationMechanism ` and register it using the `@HttpAuthenticationMechanism` annotation, so that I can control granularly where which AuthenticationMechanism is used. Therefore I disabled proactive authentication and configured policies to use these AuthenticationMechanism. The problem here is that a class named `StandardSecurityCheckInterceptor` then intercepts during the AuthenticationMechanism selection process and causes a `ContextNotActiveException`

### Expected behavior

The AuthenticationMechanism should be registered under the specified name and called without any exception

### Actual behavior

Using the AuthenticationMechanism should not result in a `ContextNotActiveException`

### How to Reproduce?

I put the relevant source code on a github gist here:
https://gist.github.com/nightloewe1/8716b62519b71e922a982624f47f3546

Steps to reproduce:
1. Create a quarkus project using Quarkus REST and Quarkus Security (might need to add this in the build.gradle.kts)
2. Add the code from the gist
3. Run the application and make a http call to it

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Darwin

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "24.0.1" 2025-04-15 OpenJDK Runtime Environment (build 24.0.1+9-30) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM (build 24.0.1+9-30, mixed mode, sharing)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.5

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Gradle 9.2.1

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#51783.title: @HttpAuthenticationMechanism with @ApplicationScoped causes ContextNotActiveException

quarkusio/quarkus#51808.body:
### Describe the bug

When using OpenTelemetry's `@WithSpan` annotation (e.g. in a normal method and/or in an `HttpAuthenticationMechanism`), MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context) values set in JAX-RS **request** filters are lost . This appears to be a regression introduced in Quarkus 3.30.5.

The issue occurs when:

1.  A method (e.g. on an HttpAuthenticationMechanism)with @WithSpan annotation executes during request processing
2. A JAX-RS `ContainerRequestFilter` sets MDC values
3. The MDC values set in the request filter are no longer present (e.g. when the access log is written)

Interestingly, MDC values set in `ContainerResponseFilter`(note req vs resp...)  are preserved correctly.

This may be related to changes in https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51641 and/or https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/43134 (cc @brunobat  ), introduced in version 3.30.5 (see release notes: https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/releases/tag/3.30.5)

Note that I have 2 PRs in my repro repo that illustrate going back to 3.30.4 _or_ not using Otel fixes things https://github.com/lloydmeta/quarkus-mdc-bug/pulls


### Expected behavior

MDC values set in request filters should be preserved throughout the entire request lifecycle.

### Actual behavior

MDC values set in request filters are lost in requests that go through methods with @WithSpan completes execution.

### How to Reproduce?

Reproducer: https://github.com/lloydmeta/quarkus-mdc-bug

Run `./gradlew test`

The tests fails with:

```
org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: MDC field set in request filter should be available. MDC contents:
    {spanId=0b45b58e38976741, response.filter.field=from the response filter,
    traceId=0126307879c80193f6577629fe9e035d, sampled=true} ==> expected: <from the request filter> but was: <null>
          at com.beachape.MdcCaptureTest.authedEndpoint_shouldHaveExpectedMdcFields_fromRequestFilter(MdcCaptureTes
    t.java:36)
```

Note that response.filter.field is present, but request.filter.field is missing.


### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Darwin Mac 25.2.0 Darwin Kernel Version 25.2.0: Tue Nov 18 21:07:05 PST 2025; root:xnu-12377.61.12~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6020 arm64

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk 25.0.1 2025-10-21

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.5

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Gradle 9.1.9

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#51808.title: [Regression:3.30.5] Using Otel results in losing MDC data set in RequestFilters

quarkusio/quarkus#51811.body:
### Description

A follow up to #50915 : that approach worked well in projects that don't have a Surefire/Failsafe configuration, but:
 - I wasn't very careful in separating configuration of Surefire vs Failsafe
 - Maven was completely ignoring it if an `argLine` was already defined in the project


### Implementation ideas

As discussed on [#dev > Maven Surefire auto-config](https://quarkusio.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/187038-dev/topic/Maven.20Surefire.20auto-config/with/566565036) : we introduce an explicitly separated property `quarkusSurefireArgLine` as suggested by @aloubyansky  ( and `quarkusFailsafeArgLine` for failsafe).
In addition, if there is no configuration at all then we write into the default properties.
quarkusio/quarkus#51811.title: Make Surefire/Failsafe configuration for Java 25 more robust

### Describe the bug

Calling `getAccessToken` with a scope fails if the scope contains a colon.

The keycloak log prints the following apparently related line:

> 2026-01-07 05:22:41,228 INFO  [io.quarkus.devservices.keycloak.KeycloakDevServicesProcessor] (docker-java-stream--840237297) Keycloak: 2026-01-07 04:22:40,825 WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-1) type="LOGIN_ERROR", realmId="3c0144ba-b96b-4441-9828-7188e850d1ff", realmName="quarkus", clientId="quarkus-app", userId="null", ipAddress="172.17.0.1", error="invalid_request", reason="Invalid scopes: x%3Ay", auth_method="oauth_credentials", grant_type="password", client_auth_method="client-secret"

quarkusio/quarkus#51844.body:
Fixes https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/51808

Created a new method on VertxMDC to copy non tracing data when we clear the MDC object reference. See `VertxMDC.reinitializeVertxMdc()`
Log scope close inconsistency only on debug. 

This is a continuation of https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51641
And also replaces https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51810
quarkusio/quarkus#51844.title: Carriover data after vert.x mdc reinitialization

quarkusio/quarkus#51845.body:
### Describe the bug

UriInfoImpl.getBaseUri will throw an exception in some circumstances with an empty host header.

Reported in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/45204

### Expected behavior

We see several different behaviors when issuing curl http or https requests with -H "Host:" or -H "Host: ", and using --http1.0, --http1.1, or --http2.

For example, if you issue an https request with --http1.1 and -H "Host:" you get:

```
2026-01-07 13:24:03,645 ERROR [io.quarkus.vertx.http.runtime.QuarkusErrorHandler] (vert.x-eventloop-thread-4) HTTP Request to /realms/master failed, error id: 43993d1b-c01c-4186-a894-89ff52464fbd-6: io.vertx.core.VertxException: For HTTP/1.x requests, the 'Host' header is required
```

Ideally all invalid situations could be handled like this - if an uncaught exception is thrown in a way that is handled by the Keycloak error handler, we instead end up with a stacktrace logged.

### Actual behavior

If you instead use -H "Host: ", there is now a stacktrace in the logs:

```
2026-01-07 13:25:10,272 ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) Uncaught server error: java.lang.RuntimeException: java.net.URISyntaxException: Expected authority at index 8: https://
        at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.jaxrs.LocationUtil.getUri(LocationUtil.java:34)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.jaxrs.UriInfoImpl.getBaseUri(UriInfoImpl.java:124)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.jaxrs.UriInfoImpl.getBaseUriBuilder(UriInfoImpl.java:129)
        at org.keycloak.url.HostnameV2Provider.getFrontUriBuilder(HostnameV2Provider.java:118)
        ...
org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
        at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$15.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:645)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.doRunWith(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2651)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2630)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor.runThreadBody(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1622)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1589)
        at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:11)
        at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:11)
        at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
        at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
Caused by: java.net.URISyntaxException: Expected authority at index 8: https://
        at java.base/java.net.URI$Parser.fail(URI.java:2995)
        at java.base/java.net.URI$Parser.failExpecting(URI.java:3001)
        at java.base/java.net.URI$Parser.parseHierarchical(URI.java:3245)
        at java.base/java.net.URI$Parser.parse(URI.java:3196)
        at java.base/java.net.URI.<init>(URI.java:807)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.jaxrs.LocationUtil.getUri(LocationUtil.java:32)
        ... 21 more
```

As far as I can tell some kind of error handling is appropriate here - we are trying to construct an invalid URI with an empty authority. 

However in prior versions of quarkus at times this didn't matter. There was a subtle change with https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/50293 - the old logic was doing https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/50299/files#diff-daa425eb55bd2d8c8aba02c3d05803f5df14b85704f2fd377eb03da20df3c7a5L134 which passed the path in the URI constuctor. The new logic in LocationUtils, uses resolve instead:

```
new URI(request.getScheme(), request.getAuthority(), null, null, null).resolve(prefix + path)
```

When the authority is null or effectively "", the new logic will fail URI validation, but the old logic will work because the path was non-empty and you get a URI like http:/// - which of cousre is not valid.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create an endpoint that calls UriInfo.getBaseUri
2. make a call to that endpoint like 'curl --insecure --http1.0 -v -H "Host: "  https://...', 'curl --insecure --http1.1 -v -H "Host: "  https://...', 'curl --http1.0 -v -H "Host: "  http://localhost...', 'curl --http1.1 -v -H "Host: "  http://localhost...' - observe a stacktrace in each of these scenarios.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

http1.0 issues seem to have been in quarkus for a while. http1.1 has this behavior starting with 3.27.1.

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#51845.title: Inconsistent error behavior with an empty host header

quarkusio/quarkus#51868.body:
### Describe the bug

The artifact ID for `io.quarkus:quarkus-class-change-agent` contains the word `class`, which led to #30807 being opened. The incorrect assumption is that the automatic module name should be based on the artifact ID, which in this case it should not be.

The module name for this module should be `io.quarkus.changeagent` to be in line with the root package of that module. It can actually get a proper descriptor though.
quarkusio/quarkus#51868.title: Incorrect automatic module name for `io.quarkus:quarkus-class-change-agent`

quarkusio/quarkus#51882.body:
### Describe the bug

Quarkus: 3.27
Maven: 3.9.8
Java:  21.0.6 2025-01-21 LTS

I am using quartz to create a `@Scheduled` job that is using `io.quarkiverse.amazonservices: quarkus-amazon-sqs` SqsClient.receiveMessage. The local environment is using localstack with sqs.

On starting the application (`mvn compile quarkus:dev`) the job will run normally. If live-reload happens, the job restarts but the aws client aborts the receiveMessage call because of an internal check looking at `Thread.interrupted()`

The thread running the job was a vertx-worker thread. Changing the configuration 
`quarkus.quartz.run-blocking-scheduled-method-on-quartz-thread: true`
solved the issue by using the quartz threads for the job. But vertx should work as well.

### Expected behavior

Live reload should properly handle vertx threads used by quartz jobs.

### Actual behavior

Aws SDK client aborts the call to sqs because of an interrupted thread after a live-reload.

### How to Reproduce?
Using this project.

[code-with-quarkus.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24511559/code-with-quarkus.zip)

1. run ./mvnw compile quarkus:dev
2. Verify job start that the thread running the job is a vertx-worker
3. Trigger a live reload

_No response_

openjdk 21.0.6 2025-01-21 LTS

3.27.1
