quarkusio/quarkus#51910.body:
### Description

Bumping `keycloak-client` version to `26.0.8` failed at #51903, after looking into it I noticed

```
org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.client.api.InvalidRestClientDefinitionException: Failed to generate client for class interface org.keycloak.admin.client.resource.RealmsResource : Multiple `@Consumes` values used in a MicroProfile Rest Client: org.keycloak.admin.client.resource.WorkflowsResource Unable to determine a single `Content-Type`.
2026-01-09T23:39:48.6425586Z 	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.client.impl.ClientProxies.get(ClientProxies.java:31)
2026-01-09T23:39:48.6426392Z 	at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.client.impl.WebTargetImpl.proxy(WebTargetImpl.java:465)
2026-01-09T23:39:48.6427486Z 	at io.quarkus.keycloak.admin.rest.client.runtime.KeycloakAdminRestClientProvider.targetProxy(KeycloakAdminRestClientProvider.java:98)
```

Keycloak Admin Client's [RealmsResource](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/integration/admin-client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/client/resource/RealmsResource.java)  does indeed have 2 `Consumes` but they do no accumulate, one is on the class level, another one that overrides it is on the method level. 
They happen to be the same values but this is just not a good example, in general, class level set the `@Consumes` for all the methods and some specific ones can override it.

I also noticed #51813, the proposed solution there where an openapi generator creates a method per every consumes works, but IMHO the REST server API does not have to be actually designed like that, multiple Consumes on a single REST resource method is what most interfaces where multiple media types are accepted on a given path would do, have a single method, for example `@Consumes("application/json+x", "application/json+y")` - having two methods here with likely very strange names like `sendJsonX` would not be great.

### Implementation ideas

I have two proposals how to improve it

-  I doubt we can expect 3rd party API providers start changing their APIs by splitting a single method with `@Consumes({"a/b", "c/d"})` into multiple methods eagerly.
 Proposal here is for REST Client to simply take the first `@Consumes`  value, `a/b` in this case - which is valid according to the method spec that can take either `a/b` or `c/d` formatted data. I.e, REST client always takes the first `@Consumes` value as a `Content-Type`
 
* Keycloak Admin Client's [RealmsResource](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/integration/admin-client/src/main/java/org/keycloak/admin/client/resource/RealmsResource.java) case is quite realistic where a main resource sets a class level `@Consumes`, and a subresource has some individual method `@Consumes` matching that of the parent resource's `Consumes` 

I can certainly open a PR to Keycloak Admin Client to remove the duplicate `application/json` but the justification for it will be rather weak, because `@Consume` does not accumulate, method level one overwrites a class level one. And it will take even if accepted many months for the `26.0.9` to be out, same as it took for `26.0.8`, we have an open issue in Quarkus that was blocked until `26.0.8` would be released...

Proposal here is to not try to accumulate class level and method level `@Consumes` - but if it is really important for Quarkus REST Client then eliminate duplicates first
quarkusio/quarkus#51910.title: Improve REST client handling of multiple Consumes

quarkusio/quarkus#51915.body:
### Describe the bug

After updating to `3.30.5` from `3.30.4`, I started noticing `Timed out waiting for graceful shutdown, shutting down anyway` errors during deployment.

Looks like the issue is caused by one of the jackson/shutdown-related commits in 3.30.5. Potentially https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/commit/825c2564ec3f1f3f5f725fbb4bf203be2a8553ed or https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51712/files

### Expected behavior

App shuts down gracefully within the timeout after SIGINT/SIGTERM is sent.

### Actual behavior

App hangs until shutdown timeout is reached.

### How to Reproduce?

[quarkus-shutdown-repro.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24554925/quarkus-shutdown-repro.zip)

Basically, the issue is reproducible when you add `quarkus-rest-jackson` extension to your project and configure `graceful.shutdown.timeout` property.

Steps:
1. build & run `./mvnw -q package -Dmaven.test.skip=true && java -jar target/quarkus-app/quarkus-run.jar`
2. stop with ctrl+c or send sigterm/sigint
3. the app does not stop on itself, only after the timeout

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

25

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.5

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

Bug is also present in `3.30.6`.

Then `quarkus.shutdown.timeout` property is not set, the app shuts down successfully. Also works fine if `quarkus-rest-jackson` extension is not installed.
quarkusio/quarkus#51915.title: (Graceful) shutdown breaks with quarkus-rest-jackson and 3.30.5

### Expected behavior

Build fails with `Error: could not open `{argLine}'`

### Actual behavior

No breakage for released and active streams

### How to Reproduce?

Build the latest Quarkus main

```
qss create app
cd code-with-quarkus
qss build
```

qss script
```
#!/bin/sh
exec jbang run io.quarkus:quarkus-cli:999-SNAPSHOT:runner "$@"
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

macOS

### Output of `java -version`

Java 21

### Quarkus version or git rev

latest main

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#51959.body:
### Description

This epic is to track the migration from Vert.x 4 to Vert.x 5 in Quarkus and across its wider ecosystem. Switching to Netty 4.2 is also part of this effort as Vert.x 5 depends on it, and this Netty branch has substantial changes compared to Netty 4.1.

### Analysis

Switching to Vert.x 5 and Netty 4.2 is not a simple dependency bump, there are modules, APIs and runtime breaking changes.

The migration effort will require a multi-step approach to migrate the direct dependency components first (likely with beta releases for downstream consumption), then Quarkus itself, then "major" projects in the Quarkiverse ecosystem.

### Vert.x 5 and Netty 4.2 migration guides

See:

- https://vertx.io/docs/guides/vertx-5-migration-guide/
- https://github.com/netty/netty/wiki/Netty-4.2-Migration-Guide

### Roadmap

The initial roadmap is the following.

#### 1. Smallrye Mutiny Vert.x Bindings

Many drivers and user-facing APIs consume Vert.x 5 through the Mutiny bindings, so it has to be migrated first (tracked by https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-mutiny-vertx-bindings/issues/1045)

- [x] New bindings generator that supports Vert.x 5 APIs
- [x] Reconsideration of what modules we should support in this project, and what modules we should drop
- [x] Alignment with the latest Vert.x 5 releases
- [x] Release a first public beta for downstream consumption

#### 2. Direct Quarkus dependencies ecosystem

Each project will need to publish beta releases when it has performed its migration.

- [x] https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-fault-tolerance
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-fault-tolerance/issues/1193
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-fault-tolerance/pull/1188
- [ ] https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-opentelemetry
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-opentelemetry/issues/542
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-opentelemetry/pull/540  
- [x] https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-common
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-common/issues/515
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-common/pull/513
- [x] https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-stork
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-stork/issues/1155
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-stork/pull/1171
- [x] https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-reactive-messaging
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-reactive-messaging/issues/3287
  - https://github.com/smallrye/smallrye-reactive-messaging/pull/3331
- [x] https://github.com/hibernate/hibernate-reactive
  - Already upgraded to Vert.x 5
- [x] https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus-http
  - https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus-http/issues/185
  - https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus-http/pull/184
- [x] https://github.com/fabric8io/kubernetes-client
  - Already upgraded to Vert.x 5 in https://github.com/fabric8io/kubernetes-client/tree/main/httpclient-vertx-5

#### 3. Quarkus "independent projects"

At this stage we will be able to create a long-running branch to prepare https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus itself to migrate.

There are 2 projects to migrate under the `independent-projects/` folder of the Quarkus repository:

- [x] https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/tree/main/independent-projects/vertx-utils
  - https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/53008
  - https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/53292
- [ ] https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/tree/main/independent-projects/resteasy-reactive
  - https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/53340
- [ ] Technical debt reduction: revisit Vert.x and asynchronous APIs usage and refactor to idiomatic Vert.x code

#### 4. Quarkus migration

At this stage we will migrate https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus itself to Vert.x 5 and Netty 4.2.

- [ ] Ensure Vert.x has a flexible dependency on Jackson (2 vs 3) (and see #52036)
- [ ] Netty 4.2 integration (see #34202)
- [ ] Address the new Vert.x `LocalContext` API (see #39710)
- [ ] Technical debt reduction: revisit Vert.x and asynchronous APIs usage and refactor to idiomatic Vert.x code
- [ ] Improve or drop our Vert.x event-bus support (see #50583)
- [ ] Remove the Vert.x clustering configuration + support
- [ ] Fix the impacted code and ensure that tests pass in JVM mode
- [ ] Fix native compilation issues and ensure that all tests pass in native mode
- [ ] Fix all integration tests
- [ ] Ensure everything passes in CI
- [ ] Evaluate performance and coordinate possible upstream fixes
- [ ] Document breaking changes and have a migration guide related to the impact of this migration

#### 5. Quarkiverse 

- [ ] Select a few priority projects to coordinate and migrate
  - [ ] https://github.com/quarkiverse/quarkus-langchain4j
  - [ ] https://github.com/quarkiverse/quarkus-cxf/issues/2062

#### 6. The big merge

- [ ] Green flag for all dependency projects to leave beta and perform proper releases
- [ ] Merge into `main`
- [ ] Celebrate!

### Initial stakeholders

Stakeholders related to the components and steps above: @jponge @anavarr @vietj @tsegismont @ozangunalp @franz1981 @DavideD @lucamolteni @geoand @gsmet @cescoffier @aureamunoz @radcortez @dmlloyd @brunobat @Ladicek @mkouba 


### Migrated artifacts version tracking

These projects have Vert.x 5 artifacts available from Maven Central starting from these releases:

- `org.hibernate.reactive:hibernate-reactive`: `4.2.1.Final`
- `io.fabric8:kubernetes-httpclient-vertx-5`: `7.6.1` 
- `io.smallrye.reactive:smallrye-mutiny-*`: `4.0.0-beta0`
- `io.smallrye.common:smallrye-common-vertx5-context`: `2.17.0`
- `io.smallrye:smallrye-fault-tolerance` (and related): `7.0.0-RC1`
- `io.quarkus.http:quarkus-http`: `6.0.0.Alpha3`
- `io.smallrye.reactive:smallrye-reactive-messaging-*`: `5.0.0-beta0`
quarkusio/quarkus#51959.title: Migration to Vert.x 5 and Netty 4.2

quarkusio/quarkus#51960.body:
https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blame/3744a04a2181f39dddf45f171d1eb4dbedb94bc8/extensions/observability-devservices/testlibs/devresource-lgtm/src/main/java/io/quarkus/observability/devresource/lgtm/LgtmResource.java#L69-L71`

There is a typo in the above log message (`scrapping` --> `scraping`) 

Actual:
```
            log.warnf("Multiple Micrometer registries found - OTLP and %s, no Prometheus scrapping required.", foundRegistry);
```
Expected:
```
            log.warnf("Multiple Micrometer registries found - OTLP and %s, no Prometheus scraping required.", foundRegistry);
```

quarkusio/quarkus#52063.body:
### Describe the bug

IntelliJ Gradle Sync NPE with Component Variants

## Summary
IntelliJ IDEA Gradle sync fails with `NullPointerException` when using Quarkus 3.25+ on Linux due to null `TaskDependencyFactory` in `GradleApplicationModelBuilder`.

## Environment
- **Quarkus Version:** 3.30.6 (reproduced), likely affects 3.25+
- **Gradle Version:** 9.2.1
- **IntelliJ IDEA:** 2025.3.1.1 (Build #IU-253.29346.240)
- **OS:** Linux 6.17.0-8-generic amd64
- **Java:** 21.0.9 (Amazon Corretto)

## Description

When IntelliJ IDEA performs a Gradle sync on a Quarkus project, it fails with:

```
com.intellij.openapi.externalSystem.model.ExternalSystemException: A build operation failed.
    null
    at com.intellij.gradle.toolingExtension.impl.modelAction.GradleModelFetchAction.executeAction(GradleModelFetchAction.java:234)
```

### Root Cause Analysis

1. **`GradleApplicationModelBuilder` has two constructors:**
   ```java
   public GradleApplicationModelBuilder() {
       taskDepFactory = null;  // <-- NULL!
   }

   public GradleApplicationModelBuilder(TaskDependencyFactory taskDepFactory) {
       this.taskDepFactory = taskDepFactory;
   }
   ```

2. **IntelliJ's Tooling API uses the no-arg constructor** when calling `ParameterizedToolingModelBuilder.buildAll()` during IDE sync

3. **The null `taskDepFactory` is passed down the call chain:**
   - `GradleApplicationModelBuilder.buildAll()` line 117: creates `ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder(project, mode, taskDepFactory)`
   - `ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder` line 190: stores `taskDependencyFactory`
   - Line 363: calls `DeploymentConfigurationResolver.registerDeploymentConfiguration(..., taskDependencyFactory)`
   - `DeploymentConfigurationResolver` line 60: stores `this.taskDependencyFactory = taskDependencyFactory`

4. **Component variants feature** (introduced in Quarkus 3.25 via commit 76f952b7c88) is enabled by default and takes this code path, which eventually triggers an NPE when Gradle tries to use the null `taskDependencyFactory`

5. **Property check location:** `ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder.isDisableComponentVariants()` (line 61-64) checks:
   ```java
   public static boolean isDisableComponentVariants(Project project) {
       final Object value = project.getProperties().get(DISABLE_QUARKUS_COMPONENT_VARIANTS);
       return value != null && Boolean.parseBoolean(String.valueOf(value));
   }
   ```
   Note: This checks `project.getProperties()` which reads from gradle.properties, NOT `project.ext` (extra properties).

### Key Code Locations

- **`GradleApplicationModelBuilder.java:87-93`** - No-arg constructor sets taskDepFactory to null
- **`ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder.java:61-64`** - Checks `disableQuarkusComponentVariants` property via `project.getProperties().get()`
- **`ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder.java:298-315`** - Component variants code path (skipped when property is true)
- **`ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder.java:362-365`** - Calls DeploymentConfigurationResolver with null taskDependencyFactory
- **`DeploymentConfigurationResolver.java:47-50`** - Receives and stores null taskDependencyFactory

## Reproduction Steps

1. Create a Quarkus 3.30.6 Gradle project on Linux
2. Open the project in IntelliJ IDEA
3. Attempt to sync Gradle project
4. Observe NPE during model building

## Workaround

**Global fix (recommended):** Add to `~/.gradle/gradle.properties`:
```properties
# WORKAROUND: Disable Quarkus component variants to fix IntelliJ Gradle sync NPE on Linux
# Remove once Quarkus fixes https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/XXXXX
disableQuarkusComponentVariants=true
```

**Per-project fix:** Add to project's `gradle.properties`:
```properties
disableQuarkusComponentVariants=true
```

**Important:** The property MUST be in `gradle.properties` (not set via `project.ext.set()` in build.gradle), because Quarkus checks `project.getProperties().get()` which only reads gradle.properties and system properties.

This disables the component variants feature and reverts to the legacy `ConditionalDependenciesEnabler` approach, which doesn't require `TaskDependencyFactory`.

## Suggested Fixes

### Option 1: Auto-detect Null and Disable Component Variants (Simplest)
In `GradleApplicationModelBuilder.buildAll()`:
```java
@Override
public Object buildAll(String modelName, ModelParameter parameter, Project project) {
    final LaunchMode mode = LaunchMode.valueOf(parameter.getMode());

    // WORKAROUND: When called from IDE sync (via no-arg constructor), taskDepFactory is null
    // Component variants feature requires TaskDependencyFactory, so disable it during IDE sync
    if (taskDepFactory == null) {
        System.setProperty("disableQuarkusComponentVariants", "true");
    }

    final ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder classpathBuilder =
        new ApplicationDeploymentClasspathBuilder(project, mode, taskDepFactory);
    // ... rest of method
}
```

### Option 2: Provide Default TaskDependencyFactory (More Complex)
```java
public GradleApplicationModelBuilder() {
    // Provide a no-op implementation instead of null
    taskDepFactory = project -> Collections.emptySet();
}
```

### Option 3: Handle Null Gracefully Throughout (Most Invasive)
Make `DeploymentConfigurationResolver` and related classes handle null `taskDependencyFactory`:
```java
if (taskDependencyFactory != null) {
    // use taskDependencyFactory
} else {
    // fallback to legacy approach
}
```

## Additional Notes

- The issue appears to be **OS-specific** (confirmed on Linux, not consistently reproduced on macOS)
  - Likely due to differences in:
    - Classloader/classpath ordering (ext4 vs APFS filesystem enumeration)
    - JVM threading/timing differences between Linux and macOS
    - IntelliJ Tooling API platform-specific implementations
- **CLI builds work perfectly** - only IntelliJ IDE sync is affected
- The component variants feature was introduced in commit `76f952b7c88` (backported to 3.25.1+)
- Setting `disableQuarkusComponentVariants=true` has the side effect of using legacy dependency resolution which had issues with dependency exclusions and dev-only dependency leaking (see issue #48992)
- The property must be set in `gradle.properties` or via system properties - setting via `project.ext` does not work

## Related Issues

- #48992 - Dev dependencies leak into production classpath (fixed by component variants)
- #17367 - NPE in QuarkusModelBuilder for Gradle composite builds (different but related)

## Impact

This breaks IntelliJ IDEA development workflow for Quarkus users on Linux, requiring the workaround to be applied globally or to every project.


### Expected behavior

IntelliJ should load the gradle project without the fuss.

### Actual behavior

Unable to load gradle project; throws NPE

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

_No response_

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#52092.body:
### Describe the bug

The default access log patterns only produce a LF in the log statements, but on Windows, CRLF is required for a line ending.

For the `quarkus.http.access-log.pattern` config property the description states that there are pre-defined strings that can be used (common, combined, long) and refers to the documentation about the full list of variables. However, when I configure something like `combined`, but with `%n` at the end, `%n` does not seem to be considered, as it prints out everything as configured, except for `%n` where it only prints the literal string `%n`.

quarkus.log.file.format uses a default that includes `%n` and this correctly produces CRLF on Windows.

### Expected behavior

Configuring  `quarkus.http.access-log.pattern` as follows, I expect the access log to include CRLF on Windows:
`"%h %l %u %t \"%r\" %s %b \"%{i,Referer}\" \"%{i,User-Agent}\" %n"`

### Actual behavior

Configuring  `quarkus.http.access-log.pattern` as follows, the actual string `%n` is logged at the end of the log statement:
`"%h %l %u %t \"%r\" %s %b \"%{i,Referer}\" \"%{i,User-Agent}\" %n"`

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Windows Server

### Output of `java -version`

17.0.16

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.20.4

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Maven 3.9.11

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#52092.title: Newline character in access log pattern

quarkusio/quarkus#52094.body:
### Describe the bug

We have full end-to-end tests for our web app with Quarkus Playwright. This works fine to tests webpages protected with OIDC security, and also for WebSockets with `quarkus-websocket-next`. We leverage OIDC Dev Services and Playwright to obtain a cookie and the browser will use this to authenticate the HTTP requests and to the WebSockets.

For another use case in this app we also leverage the `quarkus-test-security` extension. For example when we want to test some custom security without doing the full web app login to get some cookies like we do with Playwright.

However we just learned that some of our end-to-end tests with WebSockets stopped working when we enabled the `quarkus-test-security` extension.

The cookie is propagated on the WebSockets (it works totally fine in dev & prod mode). The WebSocket is also allowed to open even if it has `@RolesAllowed` annotations. However in test mode we now always get an anonymous identity on the WebSocket when we define the `@OnTextMessage` method. When we remove the `quarkus-test-security` extension everything works fine again in test mode.

### Expected behavior

Quarkus returns a security identity that's associated and not anonymous for WebSockets.

```java
@WebSocket(path = "/heartbeat")
@RolesAllowed("admin")
public class HeartbeatSocket {

    @Inject
    SecurityIdentity identity;

    @OnTextMessage
    public void onMessage(String message) {
        Log.infof("Message from %s: %s", identity.getPrincipal().getName(), message);
    }
}
```

### Actual behavior

The associated security identity is anonymous on the WebSocket, however it's properly associated with the actual identity for the HTTP requests.

### How to Reproduce?

I attached a small reproducer.
1) In dev mode you will see the message from the WebSocket coming from a user in the logs
2) In test mode you will see that the messages are propagated, however that the associated identity is null

quarkusio/quarkus#52123.title: Oracle dev services should be started during startup, not augmentation

quarkusio/quarkus#52124.title: MSSQL Dev Services should be started during startup, not augmentation

quarkusio/quarkus#52149.body:
### Describe the bug

For all quarkus versions >3.28.5, tests that are annotated with both `@QuarkusComponentTest` and `@ExtendWith(VertxExtension.class)` fail to execute with an error like (tested with 3.30.7):

```
java.util.ServiceConfigurationError: io.vertx.junit5.VertxExtensionParameterProvider: io.vertx.junit5.VertxParameterProvider not a subtype

	at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader.fail(ServiceLoader.java:559)
	at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader$LazyClassPathLookupIterator.hasNextService(ServiceLoader.java:1115)
	at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader$LazyClassPathLookupIterator.hasNext(ServiceLoader.java:1142)
	at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader$1.hasNext(ServiceLoader.java:1164)
	at java.base/java.util.ServiceLoader$2.hasNext(ServiceLoader.java:1246)
	at io.vertx.junit5.VertxExtension.<init>(VertxExtension.java:73)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Constructor.newInstanceWithCaller(Constructor.java:499)
	at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Constructor.newInstance(Constructor.java:483)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp$OfRef.accept(ForEachOps.java:186)
	at java.base/java.util.Spliterators$ArraySpliterator.forEachRemaining(Spliterators.java:1024)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$Head.forEach(ReferencePipeline.java:803)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$7$1FlatMap.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:293)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline$3$1.accept(ReferencePipeline.java:214)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList$ArrayListSpliterator.forEachRemaining(ArrayList.java:1716)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.copyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:570)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.wrapAndCopyInto(AbstractPipeline.java:560)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp.evaluateSequential(ForEachOps.java:153)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ForEachOps$ForEachOp$OfRef.evaluateSequential(ForEachOps.java:176)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.AbstractPipeline.evaluate(AbstractPipeline.java:265)
	at java.base/java.util.stream.ReferencePipeline.forEach(ReferencePipeline.java:632)
	at java.base/java.util.ArrayList.forEach(ArrayList.java:1604)
```

### Expected behavior

A test can have both `@QuarkusComponentTest` and `@ExtendWith(VertxExtension.class)` annotations.

### Actual behavior

Test with both `@QuarkusComponentTest` and `@ExtendWith(VertxExtension.class)` fails.

### How to Reproduce?

Scaffold a new quarkus project and add these dependencies:

```xml
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.quarkus</groupId>
            <artifactId>quarkus-junit5-component</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>io.vertx</groupId>
            <artifactId>vertx-junit5</artifactId>
            <scope>test</scope>
        </dependency>
```

The test

```java
import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.assertTrue;

import io.quarkus.test.component.QuarkusComponentTest;
import io.vertx.junit5.VertxExtension;
import io.vertx.junit5.VertxTestContext;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.extension.ExtendWith;

@QuarkusComponentTest
@ExtendWith(VertxExtension.class)
class IssueTest {

  @Test
  void myTest(VertxTestContext vertxTestContext) {
    assertTrue(true);
  }
}
```

is then failing. It succeeds with quarkus 3.28.5.

Reproducer: 
[vertx-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24797180/vertx-issue.zip)

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

quarkusio/quarkus#52155.body:
### Describe the bug

When upgrading from quarkus 3.29.4 to 3.30.0 the error below occurs when building the application. I strongly suspect the following change in 3.30.0: https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/issues/49474


application.properties
```
quarkus.openapi-generator.codegen.input-base-dir=target/openapi
quarkus.rest-client.client-prefix.connect-timeout=10000
quarkus.openapi-generator.codegen.enable-security-generation=false

auth.tokenPropagation=@io.quarkus.oidc.token.propagation.common.AccessToken
generator.modelAnnotations=@lombok.experimental.SuperBuilder(toBuilder = true);@lombok.EqualsAndHashCode;@lombok.AllArgsConstructor;@lombok.NoArgsConstructor

quarkus.openapi-generator.codegen.spec.openapi_file_yaml.additional-model-type-annotations=${generator.modelAnnotations}
quarkus.openapi-generator.codegen.spec.openapi_file_yaml.base-package=nl.organisation.pack.application
quarkus.openapi-generator.codegen.spec.openapi_file_yaml.additional-api-type-annotations=${auth.tokenPropagation}
quarkus.openapi-generator.codegen.spec.openapi_file_yaml.mutiny=true
quarkus.openapi-generator.openapi_file_yaml.auth.bearerAuth.token-propagation=true
```


The interface that gets generated by the openapi generator config above looks like this:

```
@Path("/foo")
@RegisterRestClient(
    baseUri = "http://localhost/api",
    configKey = "openapi_file_yaml"
)
@GeneratedClass(
    value = "openapi_file.yaml",
    tag = "bar"
)
@ApplicationScoped
@AccessToken
public interface ServiceApi {
    @OperationMarkers({@OperationMarker(
    name = "bearerAuth",
    openApiSpecId = "openapi_file_yaml",
    operationId = "foo",
    method = "GET",
    path = "/foo"
), @OperationMarker(
    name = "OAuth",
    openApiSpecId = "openapi_file_yaml",
    operationId = "foo",
    method = "GET",
    path = "/foo"
)})
    @GET
    @Produces({"application/json", "application/problem+json"})
    @GeneratedMethod("foo")
    Uni<Foo> fooBar();
}

```


### Expected behavior

The class should get generated, as the `@AccessToken` annotation is only on the interface. Right now I throws an error

quarkusio/quarkus#52170.body:
### Describe the bug

In setups with a parent span containing multiple child spans, decisions not to sample a trace get lost after the first child span, when configuring parentbased_tracedidratio. In consequence later child spans make their own sampling decision and create new incomplete traces.
 
After some debugging I think the reason is as follows:
* Spans which aren't sampled are wrapped in PropagatedSpan
* Propagated Span returns false for isRecording
* QuarkusContextStorage on closing a child span scope does not restore the parent span if it isn't recording: https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/6ea722ddbc4e1f981ce1755583c794297be7755c/extensions/opentelemetry/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/opentelemetry/runtime/QuarkusContextStorage.java#L106-L114
* With the trace context gone, "child spans" afterwards become new root spans.
 
This behavior was immediately visible after enabling jdbc tracing with ratio sampling. Since OpentelemetryDataSource#getConnection is wrapped in a child span even a trivial PUT endpoint running a single INSERT leads to loose traces for these inserts, since after getConnection the parent span is lost.

### Expected behavior

Spans Contexts with negative sampling decision aren't lost on closing child span context.

### Actual behavior

Spans Contexts with negative sampling decision are lost on closing child span context.

### How to Reproduce?

[child-span-reproducer.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24823948/child-span-reproducer.zip)

I have attached a small reproducer. It includes a usually failing test with some output of the unwanted spans. You can also run jaeger and the application and call localhost:8080/hello a few times.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.26100.7462]

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "21.0.8" 2025-07-15 LTS OpenJDK Runtime Environment Temurin-21.0.8+9 (build 21.0.8+9-LTS) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Temurin-21.0.8+9 (build 21.0.8+9-LTS, mixed mode, sharing)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.8 (originally happened with 3.30.6)

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.12 (848fbb4bf2d427b72bdb2471c22fced7ebd9a7a1)

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#52171.body:
### Description

As a follow up to #49920 , we need to similarly allow extensions to express the need for a `--enable-native-access moduleName` to be passed to the JVMs, consistently in all modes.

In this particularly issue I intend to scope the work on defining the API and make sure it works for *production mode only*, and test it for some selected extensions about which I'm aware of this particular need.

Making this work OOB in dev-mode and test-mode is something I'll follow up on separately.

### Implementation ideas

Same as in #49920

### Describe the bug

[As described](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/discussions/52020#discussioncomment-15603849) in the Quarkus 4 Roadmap, in order to properly support Maven 4 the new ModelVersion 4.1.0 needs to be addressed. As a first step running on Maven 4 is nice, but there is no real incentive to upgrade if not for the new features provided by the new model.

Currently, quarkus-bootstrap is not able to even resolve the project-version when using model 4.1

```
[ERROR] 1) Failed to determine version for project model

```

### How to Reproduce?

**Sample**
There is a Maven 4 branch with a Quarkus integration-test in one of our projects, which I plan to keep updated until everything works.
https://github.com/QualityMinds/lazyval/tree/maven-4

**Reproduce**
Switch to new model
```
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.1.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.1.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.1.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.1.0</modelVersion>
```
You might need to have a subproject which infers the version from the parent by ommiting the version or by just using `relativePath` without any coordinates. See [here](https://github.com/QualityMinds/lazyval/blob/maven-4/integration-test/java/quarkus-app/pom.xml#L6-L8)

Run `./mvnw verify`

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.6

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Maven 4.0.0-rc-5

### Additional information

**Other implications of Model 4.1**
Please see [official docs](https://maven.apache.org/whatsnewinmaven4.html) for Maven 4.
Since quarkus-maven-plugin has some integrations with the java-compiler-plugin I assume there will be implications.

In particular, **source-directories** can be described without build-helper-plugin and it seems this declaration is not covered by the old internal api (probably still used for build-helper).

**Annotation processors**, are now declared as regular `<dependency>` with `<scope>processor</scope>` . The old `annotationProcessorPaths` produces deprecation warnings. Furthermore, this dependency-type does not automatically pull in transitive dependencies anymore (in contrast to annotationProcessorPaths). APs need to shade them or users have to explicitly set all transitive dependencies to the processor classpath.
quarkusio/quarkus#52190.title: [Quarkus 4] Maven 4 - Support new ModelVersion 4.1.0

quarkusio/quarkus#52196.body:
### Describe the bug

The `io.quarkus.builder.Json` is deprecated and should be replaced with `io.quarkus.bootstrap.json.Json`.

However, there is a minor incompatibility caused by the fact that `io.quarkus.bootstrap.json.Json.JsonObjectBuilder` implements `Map` and `io.quarkus.bootstrap.json.Json.JsonArrayBuilder` implements `Collection`. In `io.quarkus.builder.Json` all `put()`/`add()` operations were part of the fluent API, so for example `Json.array().add(Long.valueOf(42))` returned the `JsonArrayBuilder`. In `io.quarkus.bootstrap.json.Json` `Json.array().add(Long.valueOf(42))` returns `true` (see also the contract of `Collection`)

This incompatibility may break existing code, and it does break the `io.quarkus.builder.BuildMetrics#dumpTo()` unfortunately.

I will send a pull request that will fix the problem in `BuildMetrics`. I will also add a disabled test that will show the problem in action. I'm not sure if we should fix the incompatibility or not. Maybe it's not worth the effort but it may lead to nasty bugs (like the one in `BuildMetrics`).

CC @aloubyansky 

This is a follow-up of https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51430.

quarkusio/quarkus#52230.body:
### Description

Agroal provides a `loginTimeout` which allows users to configure "the maximum time to wait while attempting to connect to a database":

https://github.com/agroal/agroal/blob/2.8/agroal-api/src/main/java/io/agroal/api/configuration/AgroalConnectionFactoryConfiguration.java#L39

Setting a low `loginTimeout` can help in the event of database failover events, where the connection pool can become stuck indefinitely: https://github.com/agroal/agroal/pull/149

Such an issue is being encountered by users of Keycloak: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/42256

Exposing this configuration in Quarkus will allow Keycloak to also expose it to users and allow for more robust HA configurations.

### Implementation ideas

AG-274 is fixed in Agroal 3.0, however the `loginTimeout` configuration has been around for some time, so it should be safe to expose this configuration before upgrading to Agroal 3.0.
quarkusio/quarkus#52230.title: Expose Agroal loginTimeout configuration via quarkus.datasource.jdbc properties

quarkusio/quarkus#52232.body:
### Description

This is related to https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-schwenkschuster-oauth-spiffe-client-auth/, with https://www.keycloak.org/2026/01/federated-client-authentication providing a good summary overall.

Both Quarkus OIDC and Quarkus OIDC Client should be able to acquire a SPIFFE token and use it as a client assertion to authenticate to Keycloak and any other provider that can accept such tokens.

### Implementation ideas

@michalvavrik already did a great job with supporting Kubernetes service tokens with a `ClientAssertionProvider` class.

Perhaps we can enhance this class further.
We should probably update the `Source` enum in the JWT configuration group, and add `SPIFFE`, and if it is SPIFFE, `ClientAssertionProvider` will get the token from SPIFFE, using its workload api or something else.

It may require some significant refactoring though, for example, making `ClientAssertionProvider`  an interface, the existing one loading from a Kubernetes becomes `KubernetesClientAssertionProvider` registered if the JWT token path is set, and another extension or just  a package in the oidc-common shipping a SPIFFE provider...
quarkusio/quarkus#52232.title: Support for SPIFFE JWT-SVID OIDC client authentication

quarkusio/quarkus#52238.body:
### Describe the bug

[McpResourcesService.read()](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/blob/main/extensions/devui/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/devui/runtime/mcp/McpResourcesService.java#L88) throws the following exception:
```
Error in JsonRPC Call: java.lang.StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: Range [19, 17) out of bounds for length 17
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$1.apply(Preconditions.java:55)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$1.apply(Preconditions.java:52)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$4.apply(Preconditions.java:213)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions$4.apply(Preconditions.java:210)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.outOfBounds(Preconditions.java:98)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.outOfBoundsCheckFromToIndex(Preconditions.java:112)
	at java.base/jdk.internal.util.Preconditions.checkFromToIndex(Preconditions.java:349)
	at java.base/java.lang.String.checkBoundsBeginEnd(String.java:4865)
	at java.base/java.lang.String.substring(String.java:2834)
	at java.base/java.lang.String.substring(String.java:2807)
	at io.quarkus.devui.runtime.mcp.McpResourcesService.read(McpResourcesService.java:88)
	at io.quarkus.devui.runtime.mcp.McpResourcesService_ClientProxy.read(Unknown Source)
```


### Expected behavior

expected MCP response instead of exception stacktrace 

### Actual behavior

MCP response with exception stacktrace

### How to Reproduce?

Explanation (Curtesy of GPT-5.1-Codex):

> The stack trace is coming from Quarkus Dev UI’s `io.quarkus.devui.runtime.mcp.McpResourcesService.read(...)`. That method assumes the URI you pass in looks like `mcp://<server>/<resource-uri>` and blindly calls `substring(begin, end)` to peel off the server name and the resource portion. In your call you passed only `mcp://local` (no trailing `/…`). The URI’s length is 17 characters, but the service still tries to take a substring starting at index 19, so Java throws the `StringIndexOutOfBoundsException` (`Range [19, 17)`).

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.7

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#52238.title: DevUI - MCP: StringIndexOutOfBoundsException in McpResourcesService.read()

### Describe the bug

OpenTelemetry traceId is not being propagated correctly between two Quarkus applications (A and B) when a downstream API call from A to B exceeds a 30-second response time.
When application A calls an API on application B, and B takes longer than 30 seconds to respond, the traceId is correctly logged in application A, but it appears to be lost in application B. Application B logs the following warning:
`Context in storage not the expected context, Scope.close was not called correctly. Details: OTel context before: {}. OTel context toAttach: {spanId=..., traceId=..., sampled=true, parentId=...}`


### Expected behavior

The expected behavior is that the traceId should be consistently propagated and tracked across both applications for the entire duration of the request, regardless of the response time from the downstream service.

quarkusio/quarkus#52242.body:
### Describe the bug

After upgrading Quarkus from 3.31.0.CR1 to 3.31.0 (Final), the Maven build fails during OpenAPI code generation with a VerifyError related to Jackson classes.

This issue did not occur with 3.31.0.CR1.

The same project builds successfully with:

Quarkus 3.31.0.CR1
Same JDK
Same OpenAPI Generator plugin version
The failure appears only after moving to Quarkus 3.31.0 / 3.31.1

### Expected behavior

Build should succeed and OpenAPI sources should be generated, as with Quarkus 3.31.0.CR1.

### Actual behavior

Build fails with VerifyError during plugin execution before code generation completes.

The failure happens during execution of the OpenAPI Generator Maven plugin: org.openapitools:openapi-generator-maven-plugin:7.18.0:generate

java.lang.VerifyError: Bad type on operand stack

Location:
  io/swagger/v3/parser/ObjectMapperFactory.create(...)
Reason:
  Type 'com/fasterxml/jackson/datatype/jsr310/JavaTimeModule'
  is not assignable to 'com/fasterxml/jackson/databind/Module'


Execution v1 of goal org.openapitools:openapi-generator-maven-plugin:7.18.0:generate failed:
An API incompatibility was encountered...
realm = plugin>org.openapitools:openapi-generator-maven-plugin:7.18.0
strategy = org.codehaus.plexus.classworlds.strategy.SelfFirstStrategy


Notably, the plugin classpath contains mixed Jackson versions:

- jackson-databind: 2.18.2
- jackson-annotations: 2.18.2
- jackson-core: 2.18.2
- BUT jackson-datatype-jsr310: 2.19.2

This mismatch results in bytecode verification failing when JavaTimeModule is treated as a Module.

After upgrading to Quarkus 3.31.0, a recent commit in the Quarkus Maven tooling now exports 
Jackson datatype packages (JSR-310, JDK8) into the Maven plugin extension classloader 
(see commit 9df90518be133aae54bb8975a0c86449a7611932). :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

This causes Jackson classes to be mixed between the Quarkus tooling classloader and 
the OpenAPI Generator Maven plugin’s classloader, resulting in:

java.lang.VerifyError: Bad type on operand stack
… JavaTimeModule is not assignable to com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.Module
during openapi-generator-maven-plugin execution.

This appears to be caused by the tooling exporting Jackson modules into the plugin classloader, 
leading to incompatible Jackson versions being resolved at build time.

### How to Reproduce?

`quarkus-openapi-verifyerror-reproducer.zip` unzip and run:

```
mvn clean generate-sources
```
Then change the Quarkus version in pom.xml to 3.31.0.CR1 to verify the build succeeds there.

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

fedora 43

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "25.0.2" 2026-01-20 LTS

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.31.0

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.12

### Additional information

[build-stacktrace.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24919403/build-stacktrace.log)

[quarkus-openapi-verifyerror-reproducer.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24919564/quarkus-openapi-verifyerror-reproducer.zip)

quarkusio/quarkus#52252.body:
### Describe the bug

We're experiencing a regression when using OpenTelemetry gauge callbacks in Quarkus applications after upgrading to version 3.30.x. Gauge callbacks are being executed with a null ContextClassLoader (using the system class loader), which prevents proper CDI injection of application beans within the callback.

This appears to be a change from previous versions where gauge callbacks had access to the Quarkus application class loader.

**Problem Details:**

In Quarkus 3.30.x, when we define OpenTelemetry metrics with meter.gaugeBuilder().buildWithCallback(), the callback code runs with Thread.currentThread().getContextClassLoader() returning null. This means:

1.  The thread falls back to the system class loader
    
2.  CDI injection fails for beans that depend on application-specific configurations or classes
    
3.  The issue is visible in application logs (not silent failures)
    

**Affected Versions:**

*   Quarkus: 3.30.0+ (working correctly in 3.29.x)
    
*   Extensions: quarkus-micrometer-opentelemetry
    

**Root Cause Analysis:**

Based on our investigation, it appears that the OpenTelemetry scheduler threads aren't inheriting or setting the appropriate ContextClassLoader when executing gauge callbacks in 3.30.x. This might be related to changes in how Quarkus manages thread contexts for OpenTelemetry.

**Impact:**

This affects applications that:

*   Use OpenTelemetry metrics with gauge callbacks
    
*   Depend on CDI injection within those callbacks
    
*   Need to access application configuration or beans
    

**Questions for the Maintainers:**

1.  Is this intentional behavior change in 3.30.x?
    
2.  If so, what's the recommended approach for CDI injection in OpenTelemetry callbacks?
    
3.  If not, could this be a regression in thread context management?
    

We'd be happy to provide more details, run tests, or help debug this issue. We're reporting this because we've found the workaround but suspect other users might encounter similar problems.

**You can find the workaround and steps to reproduce the issue here:
https://github.com/rgogi/quarkus-opentelemetryclassloader-issue**

### Expected behavior

OpenTelemetry gauge callbacks should execute with the Quarkus application's ContextClassLoader (QuarkusClassLoader), allowing proper CDI injection and class loading within gauge callbacks.

### Actual behavior

In Quarkus 3.30.x, OpenTelemetry gauge callbacks are executed with a null ContextClassLoader (falling back to system class loader), which prevents CDI injection and breaks application class loading within the callback.

### How to Reproduce?

[https://github.com/rgogi/quarkus-opentelemetryclassloader-issue](https://github.com/rgogi/quarkus-opentelemetryclassloader-issue)

1.  Clone the repository and run mvn test
    
2.  Tests will fail with "ContextClassLoader: NULL (SYSTEM CLASSLOADER)"
    
3.  The repository includes a working fix (PeriodicMetricReaderFix.java) that patches the issue
    

### Output of uname -a or ver

macOS 64-bit

### Output of java -version

openjdk version "21.0.7" 2024-10-15 LTS

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.30.6

### Build tool (ie. output of mvnw --version or gradlew --version)

Apache Maven 3.9.9

### Additional information

The root cause is OpenTelemetry's DaemonThreadFactory creating threads with null ContextClassLoader. The provided repository includes a working fix that patches PeriodicMetricReader to set proper ContextClassLoader. This regression started in Quarkus 3.30.0 and works correctly in 3.29.x. The fix demonstrates the issue is solvable by ensuring gauge callback threads inherit the Quarkus application's ClassLoader.
quarkusio/quarkus#52252.title: OpenTelemetry Gauge Callbacks Execute with Null ContextClassLoader in Quarkus 3.30.x

quarkusio/quarkus#52260.body:
### Describe the bug

If I use the new `quarkus-hibernate-panache` dependency (which is Panache Next, but it hasnt been renamed properly) and I do not include any reactive dependencies for hibernate (i.e. `quarkus-hibernate-reactive-panache`), I get an error when starting Quarkus saying it cannot find the class `io.quarkus.hibernate.reactive.panache.common.runtime.AbstractJpaOperations`.

### Expected behavior

The application should start, even if I do not include any hibernate reactive dependencies.

### Actual behavior

The application does not start with the exception:

```
12:51:19,486 ERROR [io.qua.run.Quarkus] Error running Quarkus: java.lang.ExceptionInInitializerError
        at java.base/jdk.internal.misc.Unsafe.allocateInstance(Native Method)
        at java.base/java.lang.invoke.DirectMethodHandle.allocateInstance(DirectMethodHandle.java:500)
        at io.quarkus.runtime.Quarkus.run(Quarkus.java:78)
        at io.quarkus.runtime.Quarkus.run(Quarkus.java:50)
        at io.quarkus.runtime.Quarkus.run(Quarkus.java:143)
        at io.quarkus.runner.GeneratedMain.main(Unknown Source)
        at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DirectMethodHandleAccessor.invoke(DirectMethodHandleAccessor.java:104)
        at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:565)
        at io.quarkus.runner.bootstrap.StartupActionImpl$1.run(StartupActionImpl.java:147)
        at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1474)
Caused by: java.lang.RuntimeException: Failed to start quarkus
        at io.quarkus.runner.ApplicationImpl.<clinit>(Unknown Source)
        ... 10 more
Caused by: java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: io/quarkus/hibernate/reactive/panache/common/runtime/AbstractJpaOperations
        at io.quarkus.hibernate.panache.runtime.PanacheHibernateRecorder.setEntityToPersistenceUnit(PanacheHibernateRecorder.java:13)
        at io.quarkus.runner.recorded.PanacheHibernateResourceProcessor$recordEntityToPersistenceUnit1760765959.deploy_0(Unknown Source)
        at io.quarkus.runner.recorded.PanacheHibernateResourceProcessor$recordEntityToPersistenceUnit1760765959.deploy(Unknown Source)
        ... 11 more
Caused by: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: io.quarkus.hibernate.reactive.panache.common.runtime.AbstractJpaOperations
        at java.base/jdk.internal.loader.BuiltinClassLoader.loadClass(BuiltinClassLoader.java:580)
        at java.base/java.lang.ClassLoader.loadClass(ClassLoader.java:490)
        at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader.loadClass(QuarkusClassLoader.java:579)
        at io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading.QuarkusClassLoader.loadClass(QuarkusClassLoader.java:526)
```

This happens due to `PanacheHibernateRecorder` directly calling the `io.quarkus.hibernate.reactive.panache.common.runtime.AbstractJpaOperations` class but it only exists in `quarkus-hibernate-reactive-panache-common`.

Furthermore, there is also the `PanacheHibernateReactiveRecorder`, which resides in `quarkus-hibernate-reactive-panache`.. and this one correctly calls the `io.quarkus.hibernate.reactive.panache.common.runtime.AbstractJpaOperations`.

To get around this issue, I would have to add the `quarkus-hibernate-reactive-panache` dependency, along with the reactive database client and define the reactive url in `application.properties`.

### How to Reproduce?

The simples reproducer is if I go to code.quarkus.io and select just the `quarkus-hibernate-panache` extension (optionally, add the `quarkus-jdbc-postgresql` extension as well). Upon running `./mvnw quarkus:dev` the application fails to start.

If I change the extension to `quarkus-hibernate-orm-panache` (the previous panache version), the application starts correctly.

[code-with-quarkus.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24935832/code-with-quarkus.zip)

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

21 or 25

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.31.1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.12

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#52260.title: Quarkus Panache Next should not require reactive classes

quarkusio/quarkus#52286.body:
### Description

Typically, Quarkus OIDC woud use scopes such as `profile` or `read` to have an access token acquired from the authorization code flow enhanced with extra access permissions.

OAuth2 RAR, https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc9396, offers a finer grained mechanism to request extra access token permissions, where a JSON data specifying the expected permissions is serialized as an `authorization_details` query parameter during a redirect to the OIDC provider.

RAR already features in the OpenId for Verifiable Credential Issuance spec, I see an MCP authorization spec enhancement request, so I guess it is time to make it easier for users to do RAR

### Implementation ideas

Right now, it is already possibly to do it with Quarkus OIDC, by using a a custom redirect filter bound to an OIDC authorization endpoint, I do it in my OID4VC branch.

But users should be able to set simple authorization details in the properties.

Perhaps we can add a `Rar` group as part of the `Authentication` group with a Map property for simple details and a Map of Map property for JSON objects.
quarkusio/quarkus#52286.title: Add support for OAuth2 Rich Authorization Requests

quarkusio/quarkus#52292.body:
### Describe the bug

I've encountered a bug when using GraalPy with Quarkus where one of the Python packages shipped as a Jar resource contained square brackets in the filename. When the resource is accessed from a Quarkus app, it throws an exception (stacktrace at the end). Outside Quarkus, the resource can be accessed fine.

The problem seems to stem from a [bug in JDK](https://bugs.openjdk.org/browse/JDK-8067436) (was closed as duplicate, but doesn't seem fixed). The suggested workaround is to use the 5-arg constructor of `URI`. I confirmed that the constructor accepts the path with square brackets.

### Expected behavior

Resource with square brackets in name is loaded correctly

### Actual behavior

Here's the full stacktrace:

### How to Reproduce?

Repo with reproducer: https://github.com/msimacek/quarkus-reproducer
It's the starter project with a bit of GraalPy code added into the `hello` endpoint. Steps to reproduce:
- `mvn package`
- `java -jar target/quarkus-app/quarkus-run.jar`
- Access the `hello` endpoint and observe the exception

For comparison, `mvn exec:java` will run the endpoint code without Quarkus and that doesn't crash.

### Describe the bug

test-framework/junit/src/main/java/io/quarkus/test/junit/QuarkusIntegrationTestExtension.java

### How to Reproduce?

```
git clone https://github.com/quarkus-qe/quarkus-startstop
cd quarkus-startstop/app-full-microprofile/
mvn clean package -Pnative -Dquarkus.version=3.31.1
./target/quarkus-runner
```

From another terminal (or a browser)
```
curl http://0.0.0.0:8080/data/serialization/json/complex-dto
```

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

Linux 6.18.7-200.fc43.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri Jan 23 16:42:34 UTC 2026 x86_64 GNU/Linux

### Output of `java -version`

25.0.1+8-LTS

### Mandrel or GraalVM version (if different from Java)

Mandrel-25.0.1.0-Final

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.31.1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

The issue seems to be introduced by https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51620.

Specifically, `app-full-microprofile` contains a class `NestedInterface` which is defined as:

```java
package com.example.quarkus.serialization.dto;

public interface NestedInterface {

    NestedInterface INSTANCE = new NestedInterface() {
        @Override
        public String getString() {
            return "Vive la résistance!";
        }

        @Override
        public int getInt() {
            return 42;
        }

        @Override
        public NestedRecord getRecord() {
            return new NestedRecord();
        }

        @Override
        public char getCharacter() {
            return Character.MAX_VALUE;
        }
    };

    String getString();

    int getInt();

    Object getRecord();

    char getCharacter();

}
```

Before https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/51620, when registering `NestedInterface` Quarkus would register all its known implementers as well, thus registering `NestedInterface$1` and as a result `NestedRecord`. Now that Quarkus doesn't register all implementers for transitively reachable interfaces, `NestedInterface$1` is not being registered along with `NestedRecord` resulting in the observed issue.

quarkusio/quarkus#52314.body:
### Describe the bug


We manually updated to **Quarkus 3.31.1** and followed the official [[Migration Guide](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/wiki/Migration-Guide-3.31)](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/wiki/Migration-Guide-3.31).

We set the packaging type to `quarkus` and added the `argLine` property for both **Surefire** and **Failsafe**:

```xml
<packaging>quarkus</packaging>
```

```xml
<plugin>
    <artifactId>maven-surefire-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>${surefire-plugin.version}</version>
    <configuration>
        <argLine>@{argLine}</argLine>
        <systemPropertyVariables>
            <quarkus.jacoco.data-file>${project.build.directory}/jacoco-ut.exec</quarkus.jacoco.data-file>
            <java.util.logging.manager>org.jboss.logmanager.LogManager</java.util.logging.manager>
            <maven.home>${maven.home}</maven.home>
        </systemPropertyVariables>
    </configuration>
</plugin>

<plugin>
    <artifactId>maven-failsafe-plugin</artifactId>
    <version>${surefire-plugin.version}</version>
    <executions>
        <execution>
            <goals>
                <goal>integration-test</goal>
                <goal>verify</goal>
            </goals>
        </execution>
    </executions>
    <configuration>
        <argLine>@{argLine}</argLine>
        <systemPropertyVariables>
            <quarkus.jacoco.data-file>${project.build.directory}/jacoco-it.exec</quarkus.jacoco.data-file>
            <native.image.path>${project.build.directory}/${project.build.finalName}-runner</native.image.path>
            <java.util.logging.manager>org.jboss.logmanager.LogManager</java.util.logging.manager>
            <maven.home>${maven.home}</maven.home>
        </systemPropertyVariables>
    </configuration>
</plugin>
```

The **Surefire** plugin executes the tests fine, but **Failsafe** fails with:

> `java.nio.file.InvalidPathException: Illegal char <"> at index 0: "C:\\path\\to\\quarkus\\app\\target\\quarkus\\bootstrap\\test-app-model.dat"`

Workarounds / observations:

* The Issue only appears in combination with jacoco-maven-plugin and quarkus-jacoco
* If I remove the Quarkus packaging (i.e., use the default `jar` packaging), it works.
* If I replace the argLine with the explicit option below (as generated by a new project from [https://code.quarkus.io/](https://code.quarkus.io/)), it also works:

```xml
<argLine>--add-opens java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED</argLine>
```

So it seems the issue is related to using `<packaging>quarkus</packaging>` together with `@{argLine}` in the **Failsafe** setup.


### Expected behavior

Execution of Integrationtests with Failsafe should work with new quarkus packageing and the configuration found in the migration guide.

### Actual behavior

The Failsafe plugin fails with Error 

>  java.nio.file.InvalidPathException: Illegal char <"> at index 0: "C:\\path\\to\\quarkus\\app\\target\\quarkus\\bootstrap\\test-app-model.dat"

### How to Reproduce?

https://github.com/ChristophKronberger/code-with-quarkus

Microsoft Windows [Version 10.0.26100.7623]

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk version "21.0.3" 2024-04-16 LTS OpenJDK Runtime Environment Zulu21.34+19-CA (build 21.0.3+9-LTS) OpenJDK 64-Bit Server VM Zulu21.34+19-CA (build 21.0.3+9-LTS, mixed mode, sharing)

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.31.1

Apache Maven 3.9.6 (bc0240f3c744dd6b6ec2920b3cd08dcc295161ae)

quarkusio/quarkus#52315.body:
### Describe the bug

In the build process while generating Grpc server code using Java 22 or Java 23, I obtain

> [ERROR] Failed to execute goal io.quarkus:quarkus-maven-plugin:3.31.1:generate-code (default) on project <X>: 
> 
>  Process exited abnormally (pid 478538) with exit code 1 with error output:
> [ERROR]  > NOTE: Picked up JDK_JAVA_OPTIONS: --sun-misc-unsafe-memory-access=allow -Dsun.stdout.encoding=UTF-8
> [ERROR]  > Unrecognized option: --sun-misc-unsafe-memory-access=allow
> [ERROR]  > Error: Could not create the Java Virtual Machine.
> [ERROR]  > Error: A fatal exception has occurred. Program will exit.
> [ERROR]  > --q-grpc_out: protoc-gen-q-grpc: Plugin failed with status code 1.
> [ERROR] -> [Help 1]

This seems to be due to [these lines](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/commit/8ea7bbbc034b75324cc225b3b7b15f6ad75a2b68#diff-d1d35e04762cd6609f8d401f5b9b18e8ded271558422e6f67327681ecae25c7e) in GrpcCodeGen:

```
        //Do NOT set this property on Java 17, as it will fail with "unrecognized option":
        if (Runtime.version().feature() > 21 && !existingValue.contains("--sun-misc-unsafe-memory-access=")) {
            sb.append("--sun-misc-unsafe-memory-access=allow ");
        }
```
The JVM Property is however unrecognized in Java 22 and Java 23 as well and should therefore be appended only for Java Version 24 onwards, similar to the implementation in [JvmChecksRecorder](https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/commit/22d9fc84e66ab33912bfdb6c4acc6240e38ca972#diff-eaafa3e2b6f3bfd8139d421a44b64be349e21180b436dde50359fa086d4e0cce)

```
    public static boolean isUnsafeMemoryAccessAllowed() {
        if (Runtime.version().feature() < 24) {
            //Versions before Java 24 would not complain about the use of Unsafe.
            //Also, setting `--sun-misc-unsafe-memory-access=allow` isn't possible (not a valid argument) before Java 24.
            return true;
        }
        RuntimeMXBean runtimeMxBean = ManagementFactory.getRuntimeMXBean();
        List<String> arguments = runtimeMxBean.getInputArguments();
        return arguments.contains("--sun-misc-unsafe-memory-access=allow");
    }
```

### Expected behavior

GrpcCodeGen should work with java 22 and 23  

### Actual behavior

_No response_

### How to Reproduce?

_No response_

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

22

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.31.1

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#52323.body:
### Describe the bug

We've encountered a regression (or change in behavior) moving from 3.27.0 to 3.30.1 regarding mutable-jar re-augmentation in restricted container environments (User 185).

When starting the container with `-Dquarkus.launch.rebuild=true`, the process fails with: `java.nio.file.AccessDeniedException: /zip-builder-files[RANDOM_ID]` or more precisely:
```
java.nio.file.AccessDeniedException: /zip-builder-files[RANDOM_ID]                                                                                                                                               
        at java.base/java.nio.file.Files.createDirectory(Files.java:700)                                                                                                                                           
        at java.base/java.nio.file.TempFileHelper.createTempDirectory(TempFileHelper.java:171)                                                                                                                     
        at java.base/java.nio.file.Files.createTempDirectory(Files.java:976)                                                                                                                                       
        at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.ParallelCommonsCompressArchiveCreator.<init>(ParallelCommonsCompressArchiveCreator.java:76)                                                                               
        at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.FastJarBuilder.build(FastJarBuilder.java:166)
```

It seems it's related to the `ParallelCommonsCompressArchiveCreator` (or the underlying bootstrap engine).

This explicitly hits the absolute root (`/`), which is read-only for non-root users in Podman/Kubernetes.
It completely ignores `-Djava.io.tmpdir`

**Our questions:**
1. Is it somehow hardcoded to `/` path and it's intended for the parallel builder, or should it respect `java.io.tmpdir`
2. Since we want to maintain a single image for different test scenarios (OIDC enabled vs disabled), what is the recommended way to re-augment in a restricted container if the root is non-writable?
3. Is there a system property we can use to redirect the parallel builder’s scratch space?

### Expected behavior

Application should start successfully with OIDC enabled via re-augmentation.

### Actual behavior

The following error shows up:
```
java.nio.file.AccessDeniedException: /zip-builder-files<random-number>
	at java.base/sun.nio.fs.UnixFileSystemProvider.createDirectory(UnixFileSystemProvider.java:397)
	at java.base/java.nio.file.Files.createDirectory(Files.java:700)
	at java.base/java.nio.file.TempFileHelper.createTempDirectory(TempFileHelper.java:171)
	at java.base/java.nio.file.Files.createTempDirectory(Files.java:976)
	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.ParallelCommonsCompressArchiveCreator.<init>(ParallelCommonsCompressArchiveCreator.java:76)
	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.jar.FastJarBuilder.build(FastJarBuilder.java:166)
	at io.quarkus.deployment.pkg.steps.JarResultBuildStep.buildRunnerJar(JarResultBuildStep.java:155)
```

### How to Reproduce?

A minimal reproducer: https://github.com/jsolovjo/quarkus-mutable-jar-reproducer
If it's not sufficient, we can share the real test project which encounters the issue.

**With Quarkus 3.30.1**

1. Build the application: 
`mvn clean package -DskipTests`
2. Build the image: 
`podman build -f src/main/docker/Dockerfile.jvm -t mutable-jar-reproducer:3.30.1 .`
3. Run the container:
`podman run --rm mutable-jar-reproducer:3.30.1`
4. Fails with `java.nio.file.AccessDeniedException: /zip-builder-files<random>`


**With Quarkus 3.27.0**

1. Change Quarkus version in `pom.xml`:
`<quarkus.platform.version>3.27.0</quarkus.platform.version>`
2. Build the application:
`mvn clean package -DskipTests`
3. Build the image:
`podman build -f src/main/docker/Dockerfile.jvm -t mutable-jar-reproducer:3.27.0 .`
4. Run the container:

INFO  [io.qua.dep.QuarkusAugmentor] (main) Quarkus augmentation completed in 1421ms
```

quarkusio/quarkus#52354.body:
We've got a slightly confused situation with test ordering config. 

We expose the folliowing properties:
- `quarkus.test.class-orderer` (documented incorrectly)
- `junit.quarkus.orderer.secondary-orderer` (only documented in javadoc)

and users can also set `junit.jupiter.testclass.order.default` (they generally shouldn't, but we'll come back to that). 

We also have a bunch of undocumented 'tweaker' properties:

 - `junit.quarkus.orderer.prefix.quarkus-test`
- `junit.quarkus.orderer.prefix.quarkus-test-with-matching-resource`
- `junit.quarkus.orderer.prefix.quarkus-test-with-profile`
- `junit.quarkus.orderer.prefix.quarkus-test-with-restricted-resource`
- `junit.quarkus.orderer.prefix.non-quarkus-test`

The [docs](https://quarkus.io/guides/getting-started-testing#testing_different_profiles) say 

> To reduce the amount of times Quarkus needs to restart, io.quarkus.test.junit.util.QuarkusTestProfileAwareClassOrderer is registered as a global ClassOrderer as described in the [JUnit User Guide](https://docs.junit.org/current/user-guide/#writing-tests-test-execution-order-classes). The behavior of this ClassOrderer is configurable via application.properties using the property quarkus.test.class-orderer. The property accepts the FQCN of the ClassOrderer to use. If the class cannot be found, it fallbacks to JUnit default behaviour which does not set a ClassOrderer at all. It can also be disabled entirely by setting another ClassOrderer that is provided by JUnit or even your own custom one.

I thought that's how things worked, but I've just read the code and decided differently. We have two orderers, `QuarkusClassOrderer` and `QuarkusTestProfileAwareClassOrderer`. The `QuarkusClassOrderer` doesn't really do any ordering, it's just a convenient mechanism to get in at the right time and set up config for tests. Since it doesn't do any test ordering, it delegates to the 'real' orderer. That has a default value of  `QuarkusTestProfileAwareClassOrderer`, and can be set with `quarkus.test.class-orderer`. 

`QuarkusTestProfileAwareClassOrderer` itself orders based on the type of test and the resources, but within that delegates to `junit.quarkus.orderer.secondary-orderer`. 

So we have the following problems: 
- If users set the `quarkus.test.class-orderer` property and they have more than one profile or test resource, their tests risk breaking by trying to run on closed applications. I just did a bunch of work in https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/52350 to catch that case when `junit.jupiter.testclass.order.default` is set, but our own config could produce the same bad effect
- What users should be using in most cases is `junit.quarkus.orderer.secondary-orderer`, but we don't document that anywhere except the orderer class's javadoc

I suggest the following: 
- Update the docs to make it clear that users shouldn't set `junit.jupiter.testclass.order.default` or they'll break both config and profiles
- Update the javadoc and guides to make it clear that setting `quarkus.test.class-orderer` is pretty brave, and it will cause problems in the multi-profile case
- Add a `quarkus.` alias (can we do that?) for `junit.quarkus.orderer.secondary-orderer` so that it shows up in https://quarkus.io/guides/all-config
- Document `junit.quarkus.orderer.secondary-orderer` in the guide. We can almost use the current words for `quarkus.test.class-orderer`, just with the different property name. 

cc @radcortez
quarkusio/quarkus#52354.title: Setting the quarkus.test.class-orderer property would break tests

quarkusio/quarkus#52389.body:
This is a follow on to #52354 and #52350. 

Ideally, this would all be less brittle. It used to be, before the test classloading rewrite, if you overrode the Quarkus test ordering, either intentionally by setting `junit.jupiter.testclass.order.default`, or accidentally by setting some other JUnit property in a `junit-platform.properties` file, it would be a performance problem; we'd start more applications than was strictly needed. Now, it's a functional problem. 

There are two problems that happen:
- Bypassed config initialisation. We use an orderer as a hook to get in at the right time and initialise the Quarkus config. This was done for good reasons (other hooks were too early, or too late), but is there another way? 
- Closed applications. We use an orderer to group execution so that everything in the same application runs together. If the orderer is bypassed, we try to run on closed applications and get all sorts of mysterious errors (such as #50661 and #50740). #52350 makes the errors clearer, but ideally they wouldn't happen. Could we support reviving a closed application? This has a number of risks; we've not architected to allow this, and supporting it creates a risk of memory leaks from not-closing-thoroughly enough, state leakage between apps and crashes caused by something staying closed (like Arc, or the shutdown context). This wasn't a problem before the classloading rewrite, because we'd just create a new application on demand if the currently application wasn't suitable, rather than pre-creating all the applications.

So it's a balance whether it's better to behave badly in the one situation (order is overridden), or risk behaving badly by trying to tolerate that situation. Raising this to track the discussion.
quarkusio/quarkus#52389.title: Allow running tests without the Quarkus orderer to work by reviving closed applications (and using different hook for config init)

quarkusio/quarkus#52397.body:
### Description

In quarkus-redoc, I use a list to represent elements of the `x-tag-groups` vendor extension.
https://github.com/quarkiverse/quarkus-redoc/blob/main/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/redoc/deployment/config/ExtensionsConfig.java#L26

The list itself is optional.
However, each tagGroup requires to have a name and a list of tags defined.
https://github.com/quarkiverse/quarkus-redoc/blob/main/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/redoc/deployment/config/XTagGroupConfig.java


The configuration aspect of this works perfectly as intended.
The documentation lacks a bit though.

https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-redoc/dev/index.html#quarkus-redoc_quarkus-redoc-extensions-x-tag-groups-name

First of all, there is no indication that `quarkus.redoc.extensions.x-tag-groups` is a list. 
I added a bit of documentation myself to make it clear to users on how to configure this:
https://docs.quarkiverse.io/quarkus-redoc/dev/index.html#_tag_groups

Second, the `name` and `tags` properties are defined as "required". Which they are not. Only if the list element is present do they need to be set.

### Implementation ideas

_No response_

quarkusio/quarkus#52462.body:
### Describe the bug

The `consumeMulti()` method seems to have issues:

* The method's javadoc in states "The returned list is a mutable copy" (line 118), but the implementation returns `Collections.unmodifiableList(execution.getMultis().get(id))`, see `BuildContext.java` class, line 138. This causes `UnsupportedOperationException` when consumers try to modify the list, such as when `ResteasyReactiveProcessor.determinePreExceptionMapperHandler()` calls `Collections.sort()` at line 1607.
* When no build items exist for an ID, `MultiBuildItems.get()` returns `List.of()` (immutable empty list, line 89) instead of new `ArrayList<>()`. This is inconsistent with the non-empty case which returns a mutable `ArrayList`.

### Expected behavior

Prevent `UnsupportedOperationException` after the list manipulation + be consistent with javadoc.

### Actual behavior

`UnsupportedOperationException` when consumers try to modify the list.

### How to Reproduce?

This was found when running the Keycloak's `./mvnw test -pl quarkus/tests/integration -Dtest=TracingDistTest#buildTracingEnabled` with `quarkus-nightly` that specifically contains changes introduced by https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/52129, which is going to be part of the Quarkus 3.32 release.

See: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/46095

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

MacOS (arm64 Darwin)

### Output of `java -version`

openjdk 21 (Temurin)

### Quarkus version or git rev

quarkus-nightly

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

Apache Maven 3.9.8 (36645f6c9b5079805ea5009217e36f2cffd34256)

### Additional information

_No response_
quarkusio/quarkus#52462.title: BuildContext.consumeMulti() returns immutable list contrary to javadoc

quarkusio/quarkus#52469.body:
### Describe the bug

If your application depends on a module that only contains `src/main/kotlin` but not `src/main/java`, and therefore no `build/classes/java/main`, and you use start Quarkus from a [main method](https://quarkus.io/guides/lifecycle#the-main-method) (e.g. because you're otherwise [unable to profile from IntelliJ](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/projects/IDEA/issues/IDEA-356434/Quarkus-wrong-path-to-quarkus-run.jar-when-run-with-coverage-profiler)), you'll get:

```
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.RuntimeException: java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException
	at io.quarkus.launcher.QuarkusLauncher.launch(QuarkusLauncher.java:58)
	at io.quarkus.runtime.Quarkus.launchFromIDE(Quarkus.java:120)

### Expected behavior

Application starts

### Actual behavior

Application doesn't start

### How to Reproduce?

1. Open the reproducer in IntelliJ
2. Run `Main`

### Output of `uname -a` or `ver`

_No response_

### Output of `java -version`

_No response_

### Quarkus version or git rev

3.31.2

### Build tool (ie. output of `mvnw --version` or `gradlew --version`)

_No response_

### Additional information

[kotlin-dependency-bug.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/25152549/kotlin-dependency-bug.zip)

#### Workaround

Create a class `src/main/java/Dummy.java` in every Kotlin-only module.

#### Insights

The underlying problem is probably that Quarkus expects the folder of every source set reported by the Gradle application model to exist, but it doesn't have to.

#### Potential fixes

We could return an empty ClassPathElement if the path doesn't exist:

```diff
Index: independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/classloading/ClassPathElement.java
IDEA additional info:
Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP
<+>UTF-8
===================================================================
diff --git a/independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/classloading/ClassPathElement.java b/independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/classloading/ClassPathElement.java
--- a/independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/classloading/ClassPathElement.java	(revision 2e3d1f2ac2480a7303f0f1f1db58ae25d13c4f38)
+++ b/independent-projects/bootstrap/core/src/main/java/io/quarkus/bootstrap/classloading/ClassPathElement.java	(date 1770479114121)
@@ -1,6 +1,7 @@
 package io.quarkus.bootstrap.classloading;
 
 import java.io.Closeable;
+import java.nio.file.Files;
 import java.nio.file.Path;
 import java.security.ProtectionDomain;
 import java.util.List;
@@ -97,6 +98,7 @@
      * Creates an element from a file system path
      */
     static ClassPathElement fromPath(Path path, boolean runtime) {
+        if (Files.notExists(path)) return EMPTY;
         return new PathTreeClassPathElement(PathTree.ofDirectoryOrArchive(path),
                 runtime);
     }
```

### Describe the bug

https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/52313 extends the set of classes that get registered for reflection in ReflectiveHierarchyStep. In case of Camel where there are JAXB annotations on some core classes (mostly for historical reasons) this brings in classes whose analysis fails because they refer to classes that are not available in class path. See the full report in https://github.com/apache/camel-quarkus/issues/8245

I am going to check whether some new means to customize how ReflectiveHierarchyStep produces ReflectiveHierarchyBuildItems would help.
